issue_title: Issue with NoRerverseMatch for 'social_django.urls'?
issue_body:
Not sure if its a misconfiguration. But on my system 
- Python 3.6.1
- Windows 10
- Django==1.11.3
- djangorestframework==3.6.3
- django-rest-framework-social-oauth2==1.0.8

I get for the facebook example from the Readme the following exception: 
`django.urls.exceptions.NoReverseMatch: Reverse for 'complete' not found. 'complete' is not a valid view function or pattern name.`

I did tracked it down that it appears at `rest_framework_social_oauth2/oauth2_grants.py: SocialTokenGrant.validate_token_request` at the `reverse` around:

    try:
        backend = load_backend(strategy, request.backend,
                                   reverse(NAMESPACE + ":complete", args=(request.backend,)))
    except MissingBackend...

the reason seams to be that at the `rest_framework_social_oauth2/urls.py` there is:

    url('', include('social_django.urls', namespace="social")),

Since in my applications url i have

    url(r'^auth/', include('rest_framework_social_oauth2.urls', namespace=settings.SOCIAL_AUTH_URL_NAMESPACE)),

with `SOCIAL_AUTH_URL_NAMESPACE='oauth2_provider'

Now to the solution:
replace the reverse above by the following: `reverse(NAMESPACE + ":social:complete", args=(request.backend,)))`

I think the same issue appears with all urls from `social_django.urls`.

So is it a bug? Or did I configured something wrongly?

issue_title: Handling standard auth-token along with social auth
issue_body:
So far, I have been using the standard Django REST token-auth, while replacing the "Token" keyword by "Bearer" to have the same header as with the django-rest-framework-social-oauth2:

`Authorization: Bearer my_token`

So "my_token" can either be a Token from the auth-token table or an AccessToken returned by auth/convert-view.

To handle both authentications, I thus created the following class:

```
class ExampleAuthentication(TokenAuthentication):

    keyword = 'Bearer'

    def authenticate(self, request):

        token = request.META['HTTP_AUTHORIZATION']
        token = token[len(self.keyword) + 1 :]

        print(token)

        # Django auth-token
        try:
            tok = Token.objects.get(key=token)
            user = User.objects.get(id=tok.user_id)
            return user, None

        except:
            pass

        # social auth by means of DRSA2
        try:
            user = AccessToken.objects.get(token=token).user

            return user, None

        except:
            return None
```

While I manage to get any logged user registered with `auth-token`, when passing an `AccessToken`, to authenticate a social user (using Facebook), I always get `AnonymousUser`

issue_title: Daphne and closing file descriptors
issue_body:
I'm trying to create an `init` script in order to manage the lifecycle of `daphne`; actually I'm using
this `jinja` template for it

```
#!/bin/sh

### BEGIN INIT INFO
# Provides:          websocketd
# Required-Start:    $remote_fs $syslog
# Required-Stop:     $remote_fs $syslog
# Default-Start:     2 3 4 5
# Default-Stop:      0 1 6
# Short-Description: Put a short description of the service here
# Description:       Put a long description of the service here
### END INIT INFO

# Change the next 3 lines to suit where you install your script and what you want to call it
DIR={{ site_web_root }}/app
DAEMON="../.virtualenv/bin/daphne"
DAEMON_NAME=awsgi

# Add any command line options for your daemon here
DAEMON_OPTS="--port 8000 --bind 127.0.0.1 {{ project_name}}.asgi:channel_layer"

# This next line determines what user the script runs as.
DAEMON_USER={{ webapp_username }}

# The process ID of the script when it runs is stored here:
PIDFILE=/var/run/$DAEMON_NAME.pid

. /lib/lsb/init-functions

do_start () {
    log_daemon_msg "Starting system $DAEMON_NAME daemon"
    start-stop-daemon --start --chdir $DIR --background --no-close --retry 1 --pidfile $PIDFILE --make-pidfile --user $DAEMON_USER --chuid $DAEMON_USER:$DAEMON_USER --startas $DAEMON -- $DAEMON_OPTS
    sleep 3
    log_end_msg $?
}
do_stop () {
    log_daemon_msg "Stopping system $DAEMON_NAME daemon"
    start-stop-daemon --stop --pidfile $PIDFILE --retry 10
    sleep 3
    log_end_msg $?
}

case "$1" in

    start|stop)
        do_${1}
        ;;

    restart|reload|force-reload)
        do_stop
        do_start
        ;;

    status)
        status_of_proc "$DAEMON_NAME" "$DAEMON" && exit 0 || exit $?
        ;;

    *)
        echo "Usage: /etc/init.d/$DAEMON_NAME {start|stop|restart|status}"
        exit 1
        ;;

esac
exit 0
```

the problem is that with the `--no-close` option of `start-stop-daemon` (inside `do_start()`) the requests hang; the strange thing is that if I start the script with an user that remains logged all is fine, when the user disconnects from the system the connection hangs.

I don't know if this is expected or if I'm missing something, by the way, there is a way to debug similar problems when happen?

issue_title: Improve CloudantException design
issue_body:
Using the ResultException class as a guide, we can improve the error handling design by 1) have specific class exceptions for many or all of the modules (i.e. `CloudantClientException` and 2) move the error messages from `error.py` and the other modules to an internal `messages.py` module to reduce clutter.

After a discussion with @alfinkel, will follow these guidelines:
(1) try and preserve Python `dict` behavior
(2) only throwing Cloudant exceptions when trying to communicate errors encountered pre-remote database interaction

`label[:, :-1] = buck[:, 1:]`

because bucket with key 40 is empty
one possible solution is to add the following program statement after line 104
`self.data = [i for i in self.data if i]`

issue_title: Test break for some ops if bound max error instead of mean error.
issue_body:
Some of the tests in test operators will break when you change mx.test_utils.reldiff to this
```
def reldiff(a, b):
    """Calculate the relative difference between two input arrays

    Calculated by :math:`\\frac{|a-b|_1}{|a|_1 + |b|_1}`

    Parameters
    ----------
    a : np.ndarray
    b : np.ndarray
    """
    diff = np.abs(a - b)
    norm = np.maximum(np.abs(a), np.abs(b)) + 1e-7
    ret = np.max(diff / norm)
    return ret
```

The difference is we were limiting average error instead of max error. However I think limiting max error is the right approach since averaging can hide error if it only occur in a small number of locations (say boundaries).

Here is the list of OP that need to be fixed:

issue_title: test_operator_gpu.test_convolution_with_type has fixed seed that can mask flakiness
issue_body:
The unit test in title have been using fixed seed to mask flakiness. Suggested action:
1. Evaluate whether the test is flaky without fixed seed. If not, remove seed. Else move to 2
2. If test is flaky, determine whether it's an actual uncaught edge case. If so, fix the operator. Else move to 3
3. If numerical instability is inevitable, adjust tolerance level appropriately.

x = mx.symbol.MyOp()

where MyOp is a custom operator written in c++, which prints "CPU" if it is run in cpu context, or prints "GPU" if run in gpu context.

issue_title: _backward_softsign activation is incorrect
issue_body:
The following test case will fail:

Adding modify the test case in https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/tests/python/unittest/test_operator.py#L5867
```
def test_activation():
    shape=(9, 10)
    dtype_l = [np.float64, np.float32, np.float16]
    rtol_l = [1e-7, 1e-6, 1e-2]
    atol_l = [1e-7, 1e-6, 1e-2]
    rtol_fd = 1e-5
    atol_fd = 1e-6
    num_eps = 1e-6
    unary_ops = {
        'softsign': [lambda x: mx.sym.Activation(x, act_type='softsign'),
                    lambda x: x / (1. + np.abs(x)),
                    lambda x: 1. / np.square(1. + np.abs(x)),

    }
    # Loop over operators
    for name, op in unary_ops.items():
        # Loop over dtype's
        for ind in range(len(dtype_l)):
            dtype = dtype_l[ind]
            rtol = rtol_l[ind]
            atol = atol_l[ind]
            compare_forw_backw_unary_op(
                name, op[0], op[1], op[2], shape, op[3], op[4], rtol, atol,
                dtype)
        # Finite difference testing
        finite_diff_unary_op(
            name, op[0], shape, op[3], op[4], rtol_fd, atol_fd, num_eps)
```

Reason: For y = softsign(x), the inputs for _backward_softsign and _backward_Activation are different: 
_backward_softsign takes (dy, x) as input, backward_Activation takes (dy, y) as input.

@nswamy

issue_title: Resources created when tags have spaces are inaccessible
issue_body:
This issue is a mix of Bravado / Bravado core so I'm filing here but happy to move to Bravado core if that is more appropriate.

# Summary:
When a resource has tags which have spaces, e.g "contact activites", its operations become inaccessible in the generated swagger client, because they must be accessed with the space (which isn't possible in python).

# Background:
We use bravado to create a client for this swagger spec: http://api.cordial.io/docs/v1/api-docs.php
In that spec, there are some resources with two word names (e.g `contactactivities`, `messagetransports`). In that spec, the tags for these have spaces in them (`contact activities`, `message transports`). See snippets below:

```json
"/v1/contactactivities":{
            "get":{
                "security" : [ {
                    "basicAuth" : [ ]
                } ],
                "summary": "Get Activity list",
                "operationId": "getActivityList",
                "produces": [
                  "application/json"
                ],
                "tags":[
                    "contact activities"
                ],
                "parameters": [
  ...
```

```json
"/v1/messagetransports/{id}":{
            "get": {
                "security" : [ {
                    "basicAuth" : [ ]
                } ],
                "summary": "Get a transport's properties",
                "operationId": "getsingletransport",
                "produces": [
                    "application/json"
                ],
                "tags":[
                    "message transports"
                ],
  ...
```

When we build a swagger client from this spec using bravado, we can't access those resouces:
```python
client = SwaggerClient.from_spec(
    CORDIAL_SPEC,
    http_client=http_client,
)
client.contactactivities.getActivityList() // Raises exception.
client.contact_activities.getActivityList() // Raises exception.

# Example exception (with some noise removed):
# ...
# File "../sales/coupons.py", line 256, in _send_to_cordial
#    client.contactactivities.addActivity(a="gotCoupon", email=email,
#  File "../lib/thirdparty/bravado/client.py", line 161, in __getattr__
#    .format(item, ', '.join(dir(self))))
# AttributeError: Resource contactactivities not found. Available resources: account attributes, account lists, automation templates, batch messages, contact activities, contact exports, contact imports, contacts, jobs, message content includes, message transports, orders, productimports, products, supplements
```

The exceptions happen because (from what I understand so far):
- `client.<anything>` works by overriding `__getattr__(self, attr)` to look up `attr` inside the resources dictionary
- bravado_core builds the resources dictionary from the tags if they exist. This means that for this spec, the dictionary has: `contact activities` and `message transports` as keys. 
- There's no way to access those without manually calling `__getattr__`. e.g It works if I do: `client.__getattr__("contact activities")`, but it seems wrong to directly access that as a user of the client.

Unfortunately I don't control the spec, so we can't just add more tags that are accessible.

# Suggestions
- Could the bravado_core create _'d versions of tags with spaces? That way you would have both `contact activities` and the accessible `contact_activities` in the resources dict. 
- Could the swagger client implement `__getitem__` so that `client["contact activities"]` works? This feels less semantically correct but is a workaround rather than directly calling the private function.
- Could bravado_core create resources for the tags and also the path its on (which I imagine is the behaviour when there's no tags). That way `.contactactivities` would work too.

I'm no expert on Swagger (this is my first API using it that I'm connecting to), so I'm unclear whether the tags property is meant to be used for creating the client resources. If it is, I can ask the spec authors to add more tags. But if not, they aren't obliged to tag things in a way my client will handle. Ideally bravado creates clients that match the API endpoints whether there are tags or not? 

If this is missing information that would be useful, happy to add more. Also, I'm happy to create a PR with what I think would help, but just want to make sure (as a first time contributor) that the general suggestion is reasonable (and perhaps, which one would be preferred).

issue_title: Voxforge dataset importer
issue_body:
I wrote an importer for voxforge dataset(~100 hour), and use 1/100 data for test, 1/100 data for dev, following is the code:

```
#!/usr/bin/env python
import sys
from glob import glob
from os import makedirs, path
import urllib2
import tarfile
import pandas
from BeautifulSoup import BeautifulSoup
from tensorflow.python.platform import gfile
from tensorflow.contrib.learn.python.learn.datasets import base

def _download_and_preprocess_data(data_dir):
    # Conditionally download data to data_dir
    if not path.isdir(data_dir):
        makedirs(data_dir)
    archive_dir = data_dir+"/archive"
    if not path.isdir(archive_dir):
        makedirs(archive_dir)
    print("Downloading Voxforge data set into {} if not already present...".format(archive_dir))
    voxforge_url = 'http://www.repository.voxforge1.org/downloads/SpeechCorpus/Trunk/Audio/Main/16kHz_16bit'
    html_page = urllib2.urlopen(voxforge_url)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html_page)
    # list all links
    links = soup.findAll('a')
    refs = [l['href'] for l in links if ".tgz" in l['href']]
    def filename_of(x): return path.split(x)[1]

    for i, ref in enumerate(refs[:3]):
        print('Downloading {} / {} files'.format(i, len(refs)))
        download_url = voxforge_url + '/' + ref
        base.maybe_download(filename_of(download_url), archive_dir, download_url)

    # Conditionally extract data to dataset_dir
    print("Extracting Voxforge data set into {} if not already present...".format(data_dir))
    tarfiles = glob(path.join(archive_dir, "*.tgz"))
    if not path.isdir(path.join(data_dir,"test")):
        makedirs(path.join(data_dir,"test"))
    if not path.isdir(path.join(data_dir,"dev")):
        makedirs(path.join(data_dir,"dev"))
    if not path.isdir(path.join(data_dir,"train")):
        makedirs(path.join(data_dir,"train"))

    number_of_files = len(tarfiles)
    number_of_test = number_of_files/100
    number_of_dev = number_of_files/100
    for i,archive in enumerate(tarfiles):
        if i<number_of_test:
            dataset_dir = path.join(data_dir,"test")
        elif i<number_of_test+number_of_dev:
            dataset_dir = path.join(data_dir,"dev")
        else:
            dataset_dir = path.join(data_dir,"train")
        if not gfile.Exists(path.join(dataset_dir, '.'.join(filename_of(archive).split(".")[:-1]))):
            tar = tarfile.open(archive)
            tar.extractall(dataset_dir)
            tar.close()
            print(('Extracting {} / {} files'.format(i, len(tarfiles))))
            print archive

    # Generate data set
    print("Generating Voxforge data set into {}".format(data_dir))
    test_files = _generate_dataset(data_dir, "test")
    dev_files = _generate_dataset(data_dir, "dev")
    train_files = _generate_dataset(data_dir, "train")

    # Write sets to disk as CSV files
    train_files.to_csv(path.join(data_dir, "voxforge-train.csv"), index=False)
    dev_files.to_csv(path.join(data_dir, "voxforge-dev.csv"), index=False)
    test_files.to_csv(path.join(data_dir, "voxforge-test.csv"), index=False)


def _generate_dataset(data_dir, data_set):
    extracted_dir = path.join(data_dir, data_set)
    files = []
    for promts_file in glob(path.join(extracted_dir+"/*/etc/", "PROMPTS")):
        if path.isdir(path.join(promts_file[:-11],"wav")):
            with open(promts_file) as f:
                for line in f:
                    id = line.split(' ')[0].split('/')[-1]
                    transcript = line.split(' ')[1:]
                    transcript = ' '.join(transcript).replace('\n', '').lower()
                    wav_file = path.join(promts_file[:-11],"wav/" + id + ".wav")
                    if gfile.Exists(wav_file):
                        wav_filesize = path.getsize(wav_file)
                        # remove audios that are shorter than 0.5s, longer than 20s.
                        # remove audios that are too short for transcript.
                        if (wav_filesize/32000)>0.5 and (wav_filesize/32000)<20 and transcript!="" and \
                            wav_filesize/len(transcript)>1400:
                            files.append((path.abspath(wav_file), wav_filesize, transcript))

    return pandas.DataFrame(data=files, columns=["wav_filename", "wav_filesize", "transcript"])

if __name__=="__main__":
    _download_and_preprocess_data(sys.argv[1])
```

issue_title: document OIDC_CREATE_USER
issue_body:
When working on PR #95, one of the things that came up was whether we should also document `OIDC_CREATE_USER` in that PR.

From the comments:

> @willkg: If you have it set to False, how does the flow work? Don't you end up with a user that's authenticated, but has no corresponding Django user? What do you do at that point?

> @akatsoulas: That's a good point. A possible scenario could be the one where the authenticate method can be overridden for the use case where a developer needs just to verify an email/user data like eg in a campaign and save them (completely ignoring the Django user). That said, probably this is a hack that does not conform with the Django flow and needs some more work/thought. An idea could be to handle cases like the above at an earlier stage of the flow. @willkg @johngian thoughts?

> @willkg: I like that there's an "escape hatch", but I think any documentation for the setting should probably include some guidance on why you'd want to use it and how to use it.

This issue covers documenting that setting along with some explanation for why you might want to do that and what you need to implement to make it work well.

issue_title: Profiling and optimizations
issue_body:
I've recently been profiling initialization of widgets in ipywidgets, and discovered that most of the time was spent in traitlets. As such, I figured I would share the profiling results, and the results of some optimizations (caching).

### Profiling
Here's the content of a basic script file that I ran through cProfile (traitlets 4.3.2):
```python
from ipywidgets.widgets.widget import Widget

for i in range(20000):
    w = Widget()
```

(the number 20'000 was chosen as it gave a reasonable execution time of around ~25 seconds.)

Here's a summary of the profiling results:

Total time: 25.87

Number of `HasTraits` instances: 40'000 (20k Widget, 20k Comm instances)

Most of the time is spent in initialization (`HasDescriptors.setup_instance`) or querying `HasTraits.traits` (with and without metadata filter).


Initialization:

| ncalls  	| tottime	| percall 	| cumtime	| percall 	| file:lineno(function)
| --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- 
|40000   	| 1.487 	| 3.718e-05	| 10.27 	| 0.0002568	| traitlets.py:961(setup_instance)
|560000  	| 1.119 	| 1.997e-06	| 5.388 	| 9.622e-06	| traitlets.py:516(instance_init)
|260000  	| 0.2687	| 1.033e-06	| 2.706 	| 1.041e-05	| traitlets.py:1690(instance_init)
|680000  	| 1.797 	| 2.642e-06	| 2.786 	| 4.097e-06	| traitlets.py:486(_dynamic_default_callable)

Query:

| ncalls  	| tottime	| percall 	| cumtime	| percall 	| file:lineno(function)
| --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- 
|60000   	| 1.095 	| 1.826e-05	| 8.536 	| 0.0001423	| traitlets.py:1407(traits)
|60048   	| 1.783 	| 2.970e-05	| 5.566 	| 9.269e-05	| traitlets.py:224(getmembers)
|60000   	| 0.9321	| 1.554e-05	| 1.581 	| 2.635e-05	| traitlets.py:1421(< listcomp>)

Common:

| ncalls  	| tottime	| percall 	| cumtime	| percall 	| file:lineno(function)
| --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- 
|100059  	| 3.082 	| 3.081e-05	| 3.082 	| 3.081e-05	| <builtins.dir>
|11417761	| 2.344 	| 2.053e-07	| 2.655 	| 2.325e-07	| <builtins.getattr>
|11142909	| 1.306 	| 1.172e-07	| 1.306 	| 1.172e-07	| <builtins.isinstance>


### Optimiation

I'll start with the profiling results after optimization, for ease of comparison (proximity in post):



Total time: 13.2

Initialization:

| ncalls  	| tottime	| percall 	| cumtime	| percall 	| file:lineno(function)
| --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- 
|40000   	| 0.0734	| 1.835e-06	| 6.655 	| 0.0001664	| traitlets.py:982(setup_instance)
|560000  	| 1.033		| 1.845e-06	| 4.918 	| 8.782e-06	| traitlets.py:516(instance_init)
|260000  	| 0.252 	| 9.692e-07	| 2.494 	| 9.594e-06	| traitlets.py:1690(instance_init)
|680000  	| 1.57  	| 2.309e-06	| 2.479 	| 3.646e-06	| traitlets.py:486(_dynamic_default_callable)

Query:

| ncalls  	| tottime	| percall 	| cumtime	| percall 	| file:lineno(function)
| --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- 
|60000   	| 0.05958	| 9.93e-07	| 0.06941	| 1.157e-06	| profile.py:68(quick_traits)
|50      	| 0.00173	| 3.476e-05	| 0.00731	| 0.0001463	| traitlets.py:224(getmembers)
|48      	| 0.00085	| 1.78e-05	| 0.00141	| 2.943e-05	| traitlets.py:1371(< listcomp>)

Common:

| ncalls  	| tottime	| percall 	| cumtime	| percall 	| file:lineno(function)
| --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- 
|63      	| 0.0030	| 4.813e-05	| 0.0030	| 4.813e-05	| <builtins.dir>
|1558177 	| 0.4486	| 2.879e-07	| 0.5848	| 3.753e-07	| <builtins.getattr>
|1283301 	| 0.2074	| 1.616e-07	| 0.2077	| 1.619e-07	| <builtins.isinstance>

Caching:

| ncalls  	| tottime	| percall 	| cumtime	| percall 	| file:lineno(function)
| --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- | --------- 
|104     	| 0.0003282	| 3.156e-06	| 0.0007341	| 7.059e-06	| functools.py:44(update_wrapper)
|50      	| 3.319e-05	| 6.637e-07	| 4.303e-05	| 8.606e-07	| <builtins.hash>)


Here are the optimizations done (in monkey-patch form):

```python
import types
from functools import lru_cache
from traitlets import traitlets

CACHE_SIZE = 2048

@lru_cache(CACHE_SIZE)
def cache_cls_dir(cls):
    return dir(cls)

@lru_cache(CACHE_SIZE)
def cache_trait_cls(cls, key):
    # Some descriptors raise AttributeError like zope.interface's
    # __provides__ attributes even though they exist.  This causes
    # AttributeErrors even though they are listed in dir(cls).
    try:
        value = getattr(cls, key)
    except AttributeError:
        pass
    else:
        if isinstance(value, traitlets.BaseDescriptor):
            return value


def quick_setup_instance(self, *args, **kwargs):
    self._cross_validation_lock = False
    cls = self.__class__
    for key in cache_cls_dir(cls):
        value = cache_trait_cls(cls, key)
        if value:
            value.instance_init(self)


traitlets.HasDescriptors.setup_instance = quick_setup_instance


@lru_cache(CACHE_SIZE)
def cache_gen_traits(cls):
    return dict([memb for memb in traitlets.getmembers(cls) if
                isinstance(memb[1], traitlets.TraitType)])

@lru_cache(CACHE_SIZE)
def cache_filter_traits(cls, **metadata):
    traits = cache_gen_traits(cls)
    result = {}
    for name, trait in traits.items():
        for meta_name, meta_eval in metadata.items():
            if type(meta_eval) is not types.FunctionType:
                meta_eval = traitlets._SimpleTest(meta_eval)
            if not meta_eval(trait.metadata.get(meta_name, None)):
                break
        else:
            result[name] = trait

    return result

def quick_traits(self, **metadata):
    if len(metadata) == 0:
        return cache_gen_traits(self.__class__)
    return cache_filter_traits(self.__class__, **metadata)

traitlets.HasTraits.traits = quick_traits
```

Notes:
 - The most time was save by caching `HasTraits.traits`.
 - There is much code in the initialization that is non-trivial to optimize, so it is where most of the time in traitlets is spent after optimization.
 - The cache checks are fast, since most of them just use `cls`.
 - The caching is robust against `add_trait` calls, since that changes the `__class__`.
 - FIXME: The cache should not be used in `cache_filter_traits` when one of the values are function types.
 - The caches can of course become invalid (this is Python after all).

I'm not sure if this should result in any patches to master, but at least I hope this can be informative.

issue_title: CalcTangentsProcess::ProcessMesh: division by zero
issue_body:
With Assimp 3.1.1:

In the file CalcTangentsProcess.cpp (line 191), in the function CalcTangentsProcess::ProcessMesh, we test if the 3 points are aligned instead of being at the same place. Because if they are aligned, the tangent and bitangent can't be computed.
Code:
   `if ( 0 == sx && 0 ==sy && 0 == tx && 0 == ty )`
should be replaced with:
   `if ( sx * ty == sy * tx )`

In my case, I had 2 points at the same place instead of 3, but the source of the issue is the same.

issue_title: pycodestyle doesn't report issues if entry in setup.cfg exists
issue_body:
It looks like the pycodestyle plugin doesn't report any issue as soon as a configuration is made in the setup.cfg


The setup.cfg contains the following lines.

```
[flake8]
ignore = D100,D101,D104,E722
exclude = .git,__init__.py
max-line-length = 80

[mypy]
python_version = 3.3
silent_imports = True
ignore_errors = True
strict_optional = False

[pycodestyle]
ignore = E722
exclude = .git,__init__.py
max-line-length = 80

[pydocstyle]
ignore = D100,D101,D104
max-line-length = 80
```

### Expected behavior

All lines longer than 80 chars should be warned to be too long, but E722 (bare excepts) should be ignored.

### Actual behavior

With a `[pycodestyle]` section in the setup.cfg pycodestyle doesn't return any warnings/errors. Lines longer than 80 chars are accepted.

issue_title: Nested or statements lead to recursion error
issue_body:
**TL;DR: Querying DynamoDB with a long list of conditions in an `Or` statement result in Python's `RecursionError`.**

With the following syntax, we can query using three or statements:

```
filter = Attr('items').contains(first_item)
filter | Attr('items').contains(second_item)
filter | Attr('items').contains(third_item)
```

We can put this in a loop as follows:

```
items = iter([1,2,3])
filter = Attr('items').contains(next(items))
for item in items:
    filter = filter | Attr('items').contains(item)
```

However, I have noticed that when the initial list is of length 119, we get the following error:

`RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded while calling a Python object`

This is due to the fact that the nested `Or` filters needs to be traversed while doing a `deepcopy`. It can be circumvented by setting the recursion limit higher. However, it would be better if we make `Or` filters with many conditions a flat object, such that we never run into a recursion error, regardless of how many conditions we concatenate.

My suggested syntax for this would be:

`filter = Or([Attr('items').contains(item) for item in self.items])`

/opt/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/botocore/signers.py in sign(self, operation_name, request, region_name, signing_type, expires_in, signing_name)
    125

--> 127             operation_name, signing_type, request.context)

/opt/conda/lib/python3.5/site-packages/botocore/signers.py in _choose_signer(self, operation_name, signing_type, context)
    172         signature_version = self._signature_version
    173         if signature_version is not botocore.UNSIGNED and not \
--> 174                 signature_version.endswith(suffix):
    175             signature_version += suffix
    176 

AttributeError: 'object' object has no attribute 'endswith'
```

After one hell of a confusing ride, it turns out that the problems is with a call to `deepcopy` in `resource()` at `session.py`:380--this ends up replacing the "sentinel" value for `UNSIGNED` with a copy of the sentinel:

```python
import botocore
from botocore.client import Config

conf = Config(signature_version=botocore.UNSIGNED)
conf.signature_version is botocore.UNSIGNED
```
prints `True`. But:
```python
import copy
conf2 = copy.deepcopy(conf)
conf2.signature_version is botocore.UNSIGNED
```
prints `False`.

I'm not sure if this issue belongs better here or on the botocore repo--I'll let you guys sort it out.

A workaround is to add `user_agent_extra='Resource'` when creating the `Config` instance, which will bypass the problematic code.

issue_body:

It currently uses ZCML for the configuration and I've managed to convert all of this to Venusian with the exception of a utility that registers my global macro template. I'm obviously missing something. How do I get something like  the following to work? Do I need to register this with the GlobalSiteManager? Or should I continue with view_config?

```
 <utility
       component=".macros.globals_factory"
       provides="pyramid.interfaces.IRendererGlobalsFactory"
     />
```

issue_body:
Hi, I found two problems trying to use djangocms-installer with this options:
```sh
djangocms --no-input --i18n=yes --languages='es,en' --utc --use-tz=yes --timezone='UTC' --reversion=yes --permissions=yes --cms-version=stable --bootstrap=no --starting-page=no --filer --db='sqlite://localhost/www.db' --django-version=stable --parent-dir=. www

# preparation of the virtualenv (with virtualenvwrapper)
mkvirtualenv project
mkdir project
cd project
pip install --upgrade pip

# install Pillow dependencies (just in case)
sudo aptitude install libtiff5-dev libjpeg8-dev zlib1g-dev libfreetype6-dev liblcms2-dev libwebp-dev

# run the next line to make it work
# sed -i "s/'filer.migrations_django'/'filer.migrations'/" "$VIRTUAL_ENV"/lib/python*/site-packages/djangocms_installer/config/settings.py

##### Expected result

I can use a config in Pipfile to control pipenv to create/look for the virtualenv in my project.

Whether a virtualenv lives in the project itself is, by nature, a project setting, not a per-invocation-of-pipenv settings. In the current setup, when I checkout my project, I want to `pipenv install`, then that's it, then it's just installed to the right position and I can start working. Rather than everyone in my team has to prepend `env PIPENV_VIRTUALENV_IN_PROJECT=1` to every pipenv command they run. It's just better to have it configured for the project.

I can also live with both, but I think it would make a lot of sense to support the same configuration in the Pipfile itself.

##### Actual result

It's not possible.

##### Steps to replicate

N/A

issue_title: Installion Command with `--pre` and Pinning is Inconsistent
issue_body:
This is not an exact duplicate of #2019, but the two are certainly related.

##### Expected result

I expect to be able to install a pre-release like I would a full-release package if there are no full-releases for a package, or else to receive a note about the `--pre` flag.

##### Actual result

When I attempt to install a dependency currently in pre-release ([`black`](https://github.com/ambv/black) in the following instances) with `pipenv` and a `>=` pin, I'm told it's unable to resolve the dependency, and that the error exists in the subgraph. However, if I use a `==` pin, then everything works. This behavior is inconsistent and confusing, and the error message is misleading. I note that I can use the `>=` pin if I specify the `--pre` tag.

```console
$ # the following work
$ pipenv install black --pre
$ pipenv install 'black>=18.4a0' --pre # installs 18.4a2
$ pipenv install 'black==18.4a0'

$ # the following do not; error output below
$ pipenv install black
$ pipenv install 'black>=18.4a0'
```

The error message (shown in the output below) did nothing to make it clear that the `--pre` flag is missing from the last two commands. What's more, if the `--pre` flag is not required with `==`, then I would not expect it to be required with the `>=` pin.

Optimally, `pipenv` should have installed the package with a warning, or else asked me whether I wanted to install a pre-release, rather than error cryptically as it did. To clarify, if my options are all pre-releases, I think `pipenv` should succeed installation, either with a warning or after a prompt. At the very least, a better error message should be provided.

I imagine the `--pre` flag as being relevant only in the context of packages that have full-releases and pre-releases. My perspective is that if I have two choices for the latest, such as 1.3.0 and 2.0a1, I think `pipenv` should ask which I want. *However* I understand that this is a feature change and changes how `--pre` exists. Given the existing behavior but also that people new to `pipenv` are unaware of the `--pre` flag, it would be nice to print a message about the flag if an distribution like 2.0a1 is available but the `--pre` flag has not been specified.

Imagined behavior:

```console
$ pipenv install black # command should succeed ...
Warning! pipenv installed pre-release 18.4a2.

$ pipenv install black # ... or else ask for correct behavior
black does not have a full-release. Install pre-release 18.4a2? y
Installed black 18.4a2

$ pipenv install imagined-package # add warning text about --pre due to alpha version
Warning! imagined-package pre-release 2.0a1 is available. Stable version 1.3.0 was installed. Please use the --pre flag with pipenv if you meant to install version 2.0a1.

$ pipenv install imagined-package --pre # no changed behavior
Installed pre-release 2.0a1
```

issue_title: Dockerfile ONBUILD is now deprecated, what should be done?
issue_body:
Hello everyone,

The reference `Dockerfile` in the root directory uses a deprecated directive and I'm opening this issue so that we decide what to do about it.

Aside: I've been away from serious python development for many years and, now that I'm dipping my toes on it again, finding and using pipenv has been a blast. Fantastic improvement, thanks a lot for the hard work! <3

So, ONBUILD has been deprecated and library maintainers started getting rid of it:

- https://github.com/docker-library/official-images/issues/2076
- https://github.com/docker-library/python/pull/314

I see a few different options to solving this:

1. Removing the `onbuild` directives but keeping the same idea/style (pro: the resulting Dockerfile will be super simple)
2. Getting rid of the Dockerfile in this repo altogether as it's a tangent to this project (So, maybe move it to a separate repo so it can evolve separately and we can get some "good enough" base images on dockerhub)
3. Crafting a fancier Dockerfile with [multi-stage builds][msbuilds]

[msbuilds]: https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/multistage-build/

For the 3rd option, I've been using the following quite successfully:

```Dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:18.04 as build

RUN apt update && apt install python3-pip git -y && pip3 install pipenv
RUN apt install -y python3-dev libxml2-dev libxslt1-dev zlib1g-dev libffi-dev libssl-dev

WORKDIR /build
COPY Pipfile Pipfile.lock /build/
RUN bash -c 'PIPENV_VENV_IN_PROJECT=1 pipenv install'

FROM ubuntu:18.04 as application

ENV LC_ALL C.UTF-8
ENV LANG C.UTF-8

RUN apt update && apt install -y -q python3-minimal

WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=build /build /app/
COPY . /app/

CMD .venv/bin/python -mapplication.main
```

It has the benefit of generating way lighter-weight final images when you need to compile things. This Dockerfile is used for a project that uses `scrapy`, `sqlalchemy` and `psycopg2` and the resulting image size is in the 240MB whereas building it without multi-stage would result in a image sized around 680MB.

However it's also quite awkward, which makes me unsure if it fits as a good "here's a Dockerfile to get you started asap" kind of thing:

1. `pipenv` is made to generate the .env in the build directory so it can be easily copied (a flag would be helpful instead of using `PIPENV_VENV_IN_PROJECT`, I can try to make that happen if you think it makes sense)
2. the application layer still has to run some `apt install` commands (pipenv generates binaries inside the .venv dir, but they aren't statically linked so not installing `python3-minimal` leads to missing libraries)

So there's that. Options so far for the Dockerfile are: Getting rid of it;  Or making it super simple but "not so good" for projects of medium or higher complexity; Or making it a bit more complex but more efficient. What do you all think?

issue_body:
I have a Python 2.7 C-extension that was made for performance, however, I noticed something strange on Windows:
When I run locally `python setup.py install` and it installs it the "egg" way, the performance of running the module 500x in a loop is:
```
     yEnc C New  took   579 ms
     yEnc C New  took   580 ms
     yEnc C New  took   580 ms
```

But when I install it as a wheel locally or via pip without a single change to the C-code, this is the result:

     yEnc C New  took   691 ms

That's a 15-20% difference.. 
I also checked macOS and Ubuntu, here it does not seem to be the case.

To reproduce run and look at the `yEnc C New` score between the 2 ways:
```
git clone https://github.com/sabnzbd/sabyenc.git

issue_title: Ignoring package_data when include_package_data is True
issue_body:
Hi, firstly, thanks for the work you maintainers do on this library :)

I could be incorrect in saying this, but it's come to my understanding that setting `include_package_data=True` in setup includes data files specified by MANIFEST.in and *only* in MANIFEST.in. At the very least, this behaviour is not clearly explained in the docs, and at the worst it is unexpected and implicit behaviour. 

This behaviour is stated in the docs:

> If using the setuptools-specific `include_package_data` argument, files specified by `package_data` will *not* be automatically added to the manifest unless they are listed in the MANIFEST.in file.

Doesn't this make `package_data` redundant when `include_package_data` is True and the files are specified by both `package_data` and MANIFEST.in? Does it really make sense to have a param called `include_package_data` which implicitly ignores `package_data`?

In summary, this is the behaviour I seem to have come across when using these options in various combinations:
 1. Including files in `package_data` with `include_package_data=False`:
  - Files specified by `package_data` are included as well as everything in MANIFEST.in.
 2. Including files in `package_data` with `include_package_data=True`:
  - Only the files specified by MANIFEST.in are included, files in `package_data` not specified by MANIFEST.in are excluded.
 3. Omitting `package_data` with `include_package_data=True`:
  - Same result as point 2.

I'm sorry if I've got this wrong, any correspondence is much appreciated :)

issue_title: Standardize logging calls
issue_body:
We've started [using the root logger](https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/4fba39ceee707f77298c9bd498d173fce6ef32cc/pykafka/simpleconsumer.py#L22) in some places, which is bad form.  We're also now inconsistent as to whether the logger will be called `log` or `logger` in the file.

We definitely need to standardize to keep things predictable.  My personal preference has always been:

```python
import logging
log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
```

Like the other logging ticket, this isn't a big thing. Just cleaning up development leftovers ahead of the release.

issue_title: Produced messages getting lost when broker is down
issue_body:
Hello, I have the following issue with pykafka, in this setting:

Running 3 kafka brokers and 1 zookeeper in docker on one machine in docker
zookeeper 172.17.0.2:2181
kafka[1-3] 172.17.0.[3-5]:9092
all default configs except kafka `config/server.properties` has 
`leader.imbalance.check.interval.seconds = 30` added, otherwise the topic leader rebalancing interval is too long - 5 minutes.
_(actually I noticed first in a real high availability cluster with 3 kafka brokers and 3 zookeepers, but that's not important I think - I reduced the problem into a local setting)_
using pykafka latest commit 0891c81 or 2.2.0, python 2.7.6, kafka 0.9.0.0, zookeeper 3.4.6, docker 1.9.1, running on Ubuntu 14.04

I send a few messages to be produced, then stop one of the kafka containers (simulating a failure) and send a few more - _(source added in gist)_. Messages for the broker that has stopped get lost.

The strange thing is pykafka reconnects all-right after a few time-outs, starts the proper worker threads, but doesn't send the messages. But during the time-out pykafka attempts to resend the messages, it seems it forgets them when the reconnect occurs _(logfile in the gist)_.
I reduced the problem to a gist here: https://gist.github.com/jonsource/4f5ec5b345f75f21b16b
with a log of a typical run, and testPykafka.py source at the bottom.

One more strange thing I noticed - the messages get lost when pausing kafka1 or kafka2. When kafka3 is paused, they get sent OK after the reconnect.

Best regards.

issue_title: Producer in tag 2.6.0.dev2 will deadlock on a call to _update()
issue_body:
This problem occurs in tag 2.6.0.dev2 commit hash c9a5f1c7627b408f1709366c5b5ec034e0ba1db9

Any call to Producer._update() that finds a non-zero number of queued_messages must always deadlock.

The Producer has a non-reentrant Lock _update_lock declared here: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/c9a5f1c7627b408f1709366c5b5ec034e0ba1db9/pykafka/producer.py#L185

The Producer's _update() function acquires the lock here: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/c9a5f1c7627b408f1709366c5b5ec034e0ba1db9/pykafka/producer.py#L228

The Producer's _update() function calls the _produce() function while it has the _update_lock here: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/c9a5f1c7627b408f1709366c5b5ec034e0ba1db9/pykafka/producer.py#L238

The Producer's _produce() function tries to acquire the _update_lock here: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/c9a5f1c7627b408f1709366c5b5ec034e0ba1db9/pykafka/producer.py#L371

This sequence of events will always result in a deadlock.

I found this deadlock while looking for solutions to hung producer problems I have been having and using a RLock seemed to fix the problem.  I don't know enough about how the Producer is supposed to work to know if using an RLock is an acceptable solution.

issue_title: Leaking connections
issue_body:
In case of when by default `RequestsClient` is used https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python/blob/master/stripe/http_client.py#L101 you are leaking connection by creating new Session object for every request. But never calling `RequestsClient.close`.

The workaround is simple you need to initialize `default_http_client` to make sure that library is going to reuse sessions between calls.  

To fix this issue, I guess there are three options:

1. Change APIRequestor code to cache the `default_http_client` if it is `None`. https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python/blob/master/stripe/api_requestor.py#L78

2. Make sure that every time you create new APIRequestor - you close the client at the end.

3. Do not create sessions in RequestsClient.

issue_title: img_as_float is slower than it should be
issue_body:
## Description
In making an example to demonstrate how to convert RGB images to Gray images I stumbled upon the following observation: the img_as_float function takes about twice as long as simply dividing the image by 255. (where the '.' is of course important). It seems to me that this should not be the case. I'm not sure how best to handle this without possibly breaking things.

See below code snippet for an example. The image is being pulled from my website.. you have permission to use it for these purposes ;)

~~~~
import time
from skimage import io, util

rgb = io.imread('http://www.artinnaturephotography.com/images/xl/kofa-mountains-yucca-sunrise-20161124_6192.jpg')

t = time.time()
util.dtype.img_as_float(rgb)
print time.time()-t

t = time.time()
rgb/255.
print time.time()-t
~~~~

Output:
img_as_float:  0.0133371353149
rgb/255. :        0.00709295272827

Ubuntu 16.04 (running on a Macbook Pro)
Python 2.7
Most recent skimage ('0.14dev')

## Way to reproduce

- [x ] Code example
- [x ] Relevant images (if any)
- [x ] Operating system and version
- [x ] Python version
- [x ] scikit-image version (run `skimage.__version__`)

issue_title: RandomDistributedScalarEncoder and SparsePassThroughEncoder are missing from nupic.encoders __init__.py
issue_body:
All the encoders are in the [__init__.py](https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/src/nupic/encoders/__init__.py#L22-L35) for nupic.encoders except for
`RandomDistributedScalarEncoder` and `SparsePassThroughEncoder` are missing from nupic.encoders __init__.py

This makes it awkward when you want to import them since you need to do:
```
from nupic.encoders import random_distributed_scalar, ScalarEncoder
rdse = random_distributed_scalar.RandomDistributedScalarEncoder()
scalar_encoder = ScalarEncoder()
```

Ideally you should only need to:

```
from nupic.encoders import RandomDistributedScalarEncoder
rdse = RandomDistributedScalarEncoder()
```

I'm also not sure about `examples/prediction/category_prediction` - is this doing anything useful? If not we should just remove the whole `examples/prediction` directory.

issue_title: -fvisibility=hidden option breaks capnproto build
issue_body:
This is in reference to the instructions found at: https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/blob/master/external/README.md

Including `-fvisibility=hidden` as part of the `CXXFLAGS` when building capnproto breaks the capnproto build. Namely, the since this option hides symbols in the shared libraries, the linker can't see them, and throws a bunch of undefined reference errors.

Here is the output of `make -j6 check` with the above `CXXFLAGS` variable definition:
https://gist.github.com/649d8492d0b30e0e9291

Here is the output of `make -j6 check` with `CXXFLAGS="-fPIC -std=c++11 -m64 -fvisibility=hidden -Wall -Wreturn-type -Wunused -Wno-unused-parameter"`: (I also took out `-std=c++11` because it's included in the capnproto makefile by default, I believe)
https://gist.github.com/47540dedeb083bed5976

Simply removing this option resulted in a successful capnproto build for me.

Is this option necessary for NuPIC to play nice with capnproto?

issue_title: Basic schema migration implementation in PG storage backend
issue_body:
I propose that we store a ``schema_version`` (simple integer) in the ``metadata`` table.

Then on startup, instead of checking the existance of the ``records`` like we do currently, we compare the schema versions.

For each missing intermediary version, we run the script ``migration_N_N+1.sql``. Each migration script would update the ``schema_version`` value.

The ``schema.sql`` would always contain the last version of the schema and end up by setting the ``schema_version`` value.

issue_title: Bibdocfile: OCR text-file is not deleted when related version of full-text file is deleted.
issue_body:
The PURGE function in bibupload deletes the old versions of a file, but the .text;1 file which contains the OCR for the previous version is not deleted.

The .text;1 file is dropped from the indexer with once the .text;2 file is created and is just stored as an unused file. As the file has no function it should be deleted.

```
-rw-r--r--  1 apache apache 122079 sep  9 10:59 content.jpg;icon;2
-rw-r--r--  1 apache apache 179315 sep  9 10:59 content.pdf;2
-rw-r--r--  1 apache apache    202 sep  9 10:59 .md5
-rw-r--r--  1 apache apache     19 sep  3 19:55 .reclinks
-rw-r--r--  1 apache apache   8871 sep  3 19:56 .text;1
-rw-r--r--  1 apache apache   8892 sep  9 10:59 .text;2
```

issue_title: BibCirculation: holding logical fields
issue_body:
Originally on 2013-03-18

In CDS, within the Books collection, it exists an [eBooks](http://cds.cern.ch/collection/eBooks) virtual collection. This is based on a tag in the metadata. However it does not exist a corresponding physical book collection. 

This is not currently possible as the information whether a book has holdings is contained only in the db.

For this reason it would be necessary to create a holdings logical fields (a bit like we have journal etc.) that can then be used to create appropriate collection

issue_title: BibEdit: introduce hotkeys for subfield operations
issue_body:
Originally on 2010-05-28

Once we have a notion of "current working field", see #89, we should
then define keyboard hotkeys for various subfield operations, such as
add subfield.

issue_title: --warn-only option for inveniomanage config
issue_body:
`inveniomanage config set/create ...` commands fail if the key already exists.

As we use these commands during installation it would be useful to have an option which prevents them from failing (print warning and return 0). We would then be able to differentiate real failures from repetitions of the same config key set/create.

My current use case is the B2SHARE installation script. I have to add some ugly code in order to be able to use the bash `set -e` command.

issue_title: Pages: Multi-language support
issue_body:
We need the pages module to be multi-language, where one URL shows content based on selected language, or redirects to the corresponding URL (i.e. [en] https://demo.tind.io/terms -> [de] https://demo.tind.io/bedingungen)

Is there any work being done on this, or have you discussed it?

issue_title: travis: fix failing tests
issue_body: Related to https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-celery/issues/53

issue_title: deposit: multiple deposits per site
issue_body:
Given the current configuration options for a deposit is not possible to have several/different deposit in one site. 

I think options like `DEPOSIT_DEFAULT_SCHEMAFORM` or `DEPOSIT_DEFAULT_JSONSCHEMA` should go together with the rest of the deposit configuration inside `DEPOSIT_REST_ENDPOINTS`.

I am not sure about other things, like templates.

issue_title: sessions: create and delete should use nested transaction

issue_title: remember config is not used
issue_body:
A remote app cannot set `remember=True` since the parameter is ignored (https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-oauthclient/blob/90083a55fe52a0678afd86e5e8cb982dae3ffbb3/invenio_oauthclient/utils.py#L89)

The parameter should be passed to `login_user` instead.

issue_title: global: check-up and clean

- [ ] Add template macros so that login buttons for each service appears the login page.

Add Python 3.6 support, Drop Python 3.3

issue_body: This was done a while back, before JSON fields and that sort of thing. Would be much neater if either the data field was stored as JSON, or at there was something equivalent to the `requests` library that allows you to pass a json parameter to POST requests.

issue_title: Trig parser can creating multiple contexts for the default graph
issue_body:
If TriG containing triples in the default graph are specified with and without braces, they end up in 2 separate contexts, e.g.:

    import rdflib

    data = """
    @prefix : <http://example.com/> .
    <> <b> <c> .
    { <d> <e> <f> . }
    """
    g = rdflib.ConjunctiveGraph()                                                  
    g.parse(data=data, format='trig')                                              
    print len(list(g.contexts()))

Prints:

    2

whereas it should contain only 1 context, i.e. a single default graph that both those triples should belong to.

I've added a unit test for this to https://github.com/RDFLib/rdflib/pull/431 .

issue_title: Icon Request: fa-masquerade-mask
issue_body:
Use Cases:

- Denote a record as being private. Perhaps an image or file in a user based site.
- A utility that enables the user to masquerade as another's role. For example if you're an Admin in a CMS and you want to test out the CMS as an Editor, you can masquerade as so until logging out.

Example, Firefox's "private mode" icon:

issue_title: urlsafe_base64_encode 在 Python2 和 Python 3 下的返回类型不同
issue_body:
在 Python2 下返回值是 str, 在 Python3 下 返回值是 unicode.

这样的话， 那些同时兼容 Python 2/3 的代码，在给会调用到  `urlsafe_base64_encode` 的函数 传递参数时， 必须显式地判断 python 版本 来决定 所传的字符串是否需要encode。会造成很多冗余和代码的丑陋。

例如：
https://github.com/glasslion/django-qiniu-storage/commit/d6984d1764dab746b4c133563494db1dbf51e4eb

issue_title: Add tags to replicas to support many replicas
issue_body:
Replicas should have the following attributes available.  This would be added as tags within the replica description, both in the API and in the SIS.  If the tags are absent, default behavior remains the same; in the config, the tags below are set to True or False:

**nofailover**: this replica should not be a candidate for automatic failover.  Used for ephemeral replicas, and multi-replica setups where there are a few designated failover targets.   If the tag is missing, the replica is assumed to be a failover target.

**noloadbalance**: this replica should not be part of any load-balancing, if HAProxy autoconfig is being used.  

**clonefrom**:  take basebackups from this replica *instead of* from the master.  If multiple CloneFrom replicals are available, one is randomly chosen for new basebackups.  If cloning using WALE or other archival backup, this tag is ignored.

**replicatefrom**: not true or false, but the IP address/hostname of another replica.  Used to support cascading replication.

issue_title: Do not wipe-out the data directory if the replica creation method returned 0, but the start failed
issue_body:
We had an issue in the field:
- the replica has been initialized successfully from a multi-GB backup
- the replica failed to start, because max_connections there were set to the lower master than on the master
- the replica is wiped out and the backup is started anew.

Should we change the logic to reinitialize the replica only if the replica method fails, and do nothing if the actual start was unsuccessful?

issue_title: pg_basebackup failed, but can't see any error messages about it
issue_body:
Postgresql 9.4
Patroni 1.4.1 using Consul
master01 has multiple tablespaces, with PG_DATA at /opt/data/psql
and tablespaces at 
/opt/data/tablespace1 through /opt/data/tablespace5

When bootstrapping a replica, we often see pg_basebackup fail, the only thing we see in the patroni log will be this:
```
Apr 29 18:30:23 slave01 patroni[30015]: pg_basebackup: child process exited with error 1
Apr 29 18:30:23 slave01 patroni[30015]: 2018-04-29 18:30:23,502 ERROR: Error when fetching backup: pg_basebackup exited with code=1
Apr 29 18:30:23 slave01 patroni[30015]: 2018-04-29 18:30:23,503 WARNING: Trying again in 5 seconds
```
followed shortly by
```
Apr 29 18:32:43 slave01 patroni[30015]: pg_basebackup: directory "/opt/data/tablespace1" exists but is not empty
Apr 29 18:32:43 slave01 patroni[30015]: 2018-04-29 18:32:43,658 ERROR: Error when fetching backup: pg_basebackup exited with code=1
Apr 29 18:32:43 slave01 patroni[30015]: 2018-04-29 18:32:43,658 ERROR: failed to bootstrap from leader 'master01'
Apr 29 18:32:43 slave01 patroni[30015]: 2018-04-29 18:32:43,658 INFO: Removing data directory: /opt/data/psql
Apr 29 18:32:48 slave01 patroni[30015]: 2018-04-29 18:32:48,300 INFO: Lock owner: master01; I am slave01
```
That last error then repeats every few minutes. The only recovery is to stop patroni, manually remove the PG_DATA directory and all tablespace directories and start patroni again. 

Is there any way to see more detailed messages about the error? I don't know if pg_basebackup sent any output to its stdout/stderr but I don't see a way to see that output since it's started by patroni.

Secondly, when patroni retries pg_basebackup, because we have tablespaces that exist, it seems to fail to remove those directories as they are not part of PG_DATA directory. Is this expected?

Depending on how the pg_basebackup failed, is it possible to restart it without losing all the replication progress? That's a rather unfortunate situation given that it can take days just to reach this point.

thanks!

issue_title: File upload doesn't work
issue_body:
I'm trying to upload a file with Swagger and Connexion, but the file part of the formData doesn't make it to my handler.

The relevant swagger.yaml is (the file is the 3rd parameter):
```
  /deployments/{deployment_id}/avatars/{avatar_filename}:
    put:
      tags:
      - "Avatars"
      summary: "Upload an avatar"
      operationId: "mypackage.controller.put_avatar"
      consumes:
      - "multipart/form-data"
      parameters:
      - $ref: "#/parameters/deployment_id"
      - $ref: "#/parameters/avatar_filename"
      - name: "avatar"
        in: "formData"
        description: "The avatar"
        required: true
        type: "file"
      responses:
        200:
          description: "Avatar updated"
        201:
          description: "Avatar uploaded"
```

My handler at `mypackage.controller.put_avatar` receives only the first two parameters, and was expecting: deployment_id, avatar_filename, avatar:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/mysourcedir/eggs/Flask-0.10.1-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1817, in wsgi_app
    response = self.full_dispatch_request()
  File "/mysourcedir/eggs/Flask-0.10.1-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1477, in full_dispatch_request
    rv = self.handle_user_exception(e)
  File "/mysourcedir/eggs/Flask-0.10.1-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1381, in handle_user_exception
    reraise(exc_type, exc_value, tb)
  File "/mysourcedir/eggs/Flask-0.10.1-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1475, in full_dispatch_request
    rv = self.dispatch_request()
  File "/mysourcedir/eggs/Flask-0.10.1-py2.7.egg/flask/app.py", line 1461, in dispatch_request
    return self.view_functions[rule.endpoint](**req.view_args)
  File "/mysourcedir/eggs/connexion-1.0.61-py2.7.egg/connexion/decorators/validation.py", line 192, in wrapper
    response = function(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/mysourcedir/eggs/connexion-1.0.61-py2.7.egg/connexion/decorators/produces.py", line 117, in wrapper
    data, status_code, headers = self.get_full_response(function(*args, **kwargs))
  File "/mysourcedir/eggs/connexion-1.0.61-py2.7.egg/connexion/decorators/response.py", line 70, in wrapper
    result = function(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/mysourcedir/eggs/connexion-1.0.61-py2.7.egg/connexion/decorators/parameter.py", line 125, in wrapper
    return function(*args, **kwargs)
TypeError: put_avatar() takes exactly 3 arguments (2 given)
```

I think this is an error in https://github.com/zalando/connexion/blob/master/connexion/decorators/parameter.py#L51.

In defining the wrapper, we take information from the flask request and provide it to the handler function. However, the `flask.request.files` are all missed.
`flask.request.form.items()` doesn't contain my file, because I think flask moves this to `flask.request.files`, which does contain my file.

I think this wrapper function should have an extra step:
- body parameters (json)
- query arguments
- form arguments
- **files**

issue_title: hbmqtt accepts malformed packets but treats them as NOOP
issue_body:
MQTT brokers and clients must close the network connection in case of protocol violation according to 
> Unless stated otherwise, if either the Server or Client encounters a protocol violation, it MUST close the Network Connection on which it received that Control Packet which caused the protocol violation [MQTT-4.8.0-1]

However, I have noticed that hbmqtt handles malformed packets or illegally sent packets in some cases by keeping the network connection open but ignoring all other subsequently received packets.  In other cases, illegally sent packets are ignored but normal operation is continued. 

Here are a few example with corresponding specification statements:

-  Subscriptions with QoS = 3 are acknowledged but treated as NOOP, i.e. a connection still persists afterwards, but messages published to the corresponding topic are not forwarded to subscribers with QoS 3. This violates: 

> The Server MUST treat a SUBSCRIBE packet as malformed and close the Network Connection if any of Reserved bits in the payload are non-zero, or QoS is not 0,1 or 2 [MQTT-3-8.3-4].

- The same holds for publishing with QoS = 3 which violates:

> A PUBLISH Packet MUST NOT have both QoS bits set to 1. If a Server or Client receives a PUBLISH Packet which has both QoS bits set to 1 it MUST close the Network Connection [MQTT-3.3.1-4].

- If a packet other than CONNECT is sent as first packet, hbmqtt enters a state where all packets are ignored, but the network connection is kept open. This violates:

> [MQTT-3.1.0-1] After a Network Connection is established by a Client to a Server, the first Packet
sent from the Client to the Server MUST be a CONNECT Packet.

and [MQTT-4.8.0-1] cited above.

- If a CONNECT packet is sent while being connected, it is ignored and normal operation is continued which is a violation of: 

> A Client can only send the CONNECT Packet once over a Network Connection. The Server MUST
process a second CONNECT Packet sent from a Client as a protocol violation and disconnect the Client [MQTT-3.1.0-2].

- Another error that is debatable is the following:
 hbmqtt treats PUBLISH packets with the DUP-flag set to true and QoS = 0 as NOOP as well, i.e. neither the published message is forwarded to subscribed clients, nor is the network connection closed. Strictly speaking, this violates

> The DUP flag MUST be set to 0 for all QoS 0 messages [MQTT-3.3.1-2]

and [MQTT-4.8.0-1] cited above.

Hence, the network connection should probably be closed. However, I have looked at four other implementations which all simply ignore the DUP-flag and forward the message. This is probably better suited to the needs of users although it somehow violates the specification.

issue_title: <depend> declaration tag handling
issue_body:
In #32 / v1.4.10 a check for sorted build_depend, exec_depend, etc values was introduced. However it does not handle use of the <depend> tag well, which is a shortcut for specifying build_depend, build_export_depend and exec_depend for the same package (http://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0140.html#depend-multiple). Apparent from making it easier to read corresponding package.xml files as they become shorter, it is also less prone to forgetting one of the tags.

However, as this tag is (apparently) resolved to build_depend, build_export_depend and exec_depend at loading, the following valid package.xml causes issues with the declaration tag sort check:
```
<build_depend>only_for_compilation</build_depend>

<depend>a_pkg</depend>
<depend>yet_another_pkg</depend>
```
It is impossible to order these three lines to satisfy the check, unless the <depend> tags are written as their three underlying tags (i.e. adding another 4 lines).

Short of parsing package.xml in catkin_lint itself or declaring the use of <depend> unwanted, I don't know how to solve this issue.

issue_title: Use entities data to generate test case parsers dynamically
issue_body:
The [`entities.py`](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/blob/master/openfisca_france/entities.py) module contains data about entities and roles of persons. It should be the single source of truth for these data.

But at some other places roles are hard-coded like in the [test case validator](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/blob/master/openfisca_france/scenarios.py#L127) where we see `foyers_fiscaux = ` or `declarants =`, etc..

I think they could be generated dynamically, based on the `entities.py` data, then moved to the Core as they will become generic.

This could be useful for Tunisia and Senegal and reduce the boilerplate when initiating a new country.

I don't intend to start doing it for the moment, this issue is for the record.

issue_title: print warning about geopandas if `getattr(df, '_geometry_column')` error
Ability to change Sprint state
Cookie based authentication in addition to current available ones
issue_body:
Implement cookie based authentication as described in https://developer.atlassian.com/jiradev/jira-apis/jira-rest-apis/jira-rest-api-tutorials/jira-rest-api-example-cookie-based-authentication

The idea is to take the user credentials (username and password), authenticate using the credentials in the body of a POST only, as described in the link above, and then let the cookies in subsequent requests be used for authorization.

Further, upon cookie expiration, re-authentication should work transparently.

As a side note, we've been requested by Atlassian to use this method instead of Basic Authentication due to heavy additional resource utilization when using Basic Authentication, because each request requires the authorization to be processed. Cookie based authorization is thus better from a performance point of view (as perceived by the server).

Also, please note this is different from the use case described in #72 "Support login by cookies. Don't need to take username password every time" or #324 "Cookies based authentication support", because our use case doesn't have to do with persisting the cookie. We still want to accept username and password and have everything working as similar as before as possible.

Finally, the idea of removing authorization from every request, as described in https://github.com/pycontribs/jira/issues/324#issuecomment-292647132, would probably achieve similar performance improvements, but wouldn't lead to automatic re-authentication when the cookie expires, as far as I can tell.

I've implemented this authentication and will follow with a PR shortly.

issue_title: evm: trace does not record terminating instructions
issue_body:
evm records the execution trace via a handler for the `did_execute_instruction` event. that event is emitted after the instruction implementation function returns. for some instructions like REVERT, the impl function never returns, as an exception is raised. this means that instruction is missed in the trace.

issue_title: Autobatching fails: exec.cc:646 SIGSEGV, abptr == 0 but active_batched[abptr-1] accessed
Simplify/robustify LogSoftmax backward calc
issue_body:
The gradient value for logsoftmax should be something like "grad - sum(grad)*softmax(x)" (which Theano and TensorFlow use).
```
z.t<0>().device(*dev.edevice) = fx.t<1>().binaryExpr(dEdf.t<1>(), FWeightedError()).sum();
Eigen::array<int, 1> bcast; bcast[0] = fx.d.rows();
dEdxi.t<1>().device(*dev.edevice) += fx.t<1>().exp() * -z.t<1>().broadcast(bcast) + dEdf.t<1>();
```
or, in cnn1
`float off_diag_sum = -(*fx).binaryExpr(*dEdf, FWeightedError()).sum();`
where FWeightedError calculates `expf(t) * d / expf(t)`, which is `d` in most cases and **nan** in cases where t is large and negative, (which means the nan will propagate and clip_gradients will abort the program).

Simplifying this to the moral equivalent of
`float off_diag_sum = -(*dEdf).sum();`
would remove the occurrence of nans in some cases.

issue_title: Airborne plotting broken
issue_body:
I just realized that the ```AirborneRadarDisplay.plot_sweep_grid``` was inadvertently broken when a combination of things occurred. 
First, AirborneRadarDisplay was made a subclass of RadarDisplay, thus some methods are superseded. 

Second, LazyLoad _get_x_y_z calculates Cartesian space based upon ```pyart.core.transforms.antenna_vectors_to_cartesian```. For a tail radar such as the NOAA P-3 (for which this class was created), the calculation should actually use ```pyart.core.transforms.antenna_to_cartesian_track_relative``` which uses a coordinate transformation for aircraft using rotation.

How do you think this should be fixed? A conditional statement could be placed somewhere along the path (e.g. in ```pyart.core.radar.get_gate_x_y_z``` that would check the platform and assign the correct lazy load gate calculation.

issue_title: Gates not collected in Sigmet files not properly marked.
issue_body: When reading a Sigmet file with Py-ART, if the number of bins (gates) changes within a sweep, rays which have few gates than the first ray in the sweep will not be properly masked.  Specifically for the "short" rays all gates beyond the last collected gate will have a value corresponding to the field specific conversion of a raw value of 1.  For 2-byte velocities field this is -327.67 which can be seen in XSAPR RHI files (for example the file XNW110524015547.RAW7KB6).  The number of valid gates in each ray is recorded in the "nbins" key in the metadata returned by the SigmetFile.read_data() method which is extracted from the ray headers.  This parameter should be used to mask gates in each ray which were not collected either in the **read_sigmet** function or more likely in the **SigmetFile** class itself.

issue_title: Query.fetch() w/o limit returns different results than w/ large limit.
Synchronous query API hides job
issue_body:
Under the covers, the resources returned by [synchronous `jobs/query` API](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/v2/jobs/query) and the [follow-on `jobs/getQueryResults` API](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/v2/jobs/getQueryResults) obscure most information found in the underlying [`jobs` resource](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/v2/jobs#resource) for the query job.

I'm up against this issue while trying to implement `Client.list_jobs`, which calls the [`jobs/list` API](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/v2/jobs/list) and tries to return instances of the appropriate job class.  That API call returns job resources for synchronous queries which are effectively indistinguishable from those created for asynchronous queries (I've only been able to tell them apart by checking that the `datasetId` of the `destinationTable` does not match any of the "public" datasets for the project).

Because of this discrepancy, I believe that the current `jobs.RunSyncQueryJob` class is misnamed:  instead, it should be called something like `query.QueryResults`:  this new/renamed class would not pretend to be a job, but instead would have a `job` accessor which created/returned a `jobs.QueryJob` instance (which would be renamed from the current `jobs.RunAsyncQueryJob`).  `Client.list_jobs` would then always return instances of `jobs.QueryJob`, even for synchronous queries.

@dhermes, @jgeewax please comment if you disagree with this conclusion.

issue_title: bigquery dataset should not always take project argument from client
issue_body:
The project in which a dataset resides is not necessarily the project in which jobs involving the dataset will be executed. For example, if querying the bigquery public data hacker news dataset the following will error

```
client = bigquery.Client(project='bigquery-public-data')
dataset = client.dataset('hacker_news')
client.extract_table_storage(..., dataset.table('comments'))
```

Because the user can't create a job in the project 'bigquery-public-data'. Instead the fairly convoluted:

```
client = bigquery.Client(project='my-project')
client.extract_table_storage(..., bigquery.Client(project='bigquery-public-data').dataset('hacker_news').table('comments'))
```

Despite the fact that the client involved will never be used.

The proposed fix is to add a kwarg `project` to the Dataset class which overrides the originating project.

issue_title: PROPOSAL: Storage API consistency fix
issue_body:
Guiding principles:
-   [x] Getters and setters should **never** make HTTP requests. Lazy loading is OK,
  but only when it involves instance creation / other local (non-network bound) behavior. For [example](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/f73ad7e8fc877e14e2f9c0b4ec42430f2396f770/gcloud/storage/bucket.py#L139-L144), in `Bucket.acl` this already happens:
  
  ``` python
  @property
  def acl(self):
      """Create our ACL on demand."""
      if self._acl is None:
          self._acl = BucketACL(self)
      return self._acl
  ```
-   [ ] More generally HTTP requests should be limited to explicit method calls. This also rules out constructors loading data.
-   [ ] `Blob`, `Bucket`, and `*ACL` (the only nouns) instances should have `load()`, `exists()`, `create()`, `delete()`, and `update()` methods. This design gives rise to code like
  
  ``` python
  blob = Blob('/remote/path.txt', bucket=bucket, properties=properties)
  try:
      blob.load()  # Just metadata
  except NotFound:
      blob.upload_from_file(filename)  # Sends metadata from properties
  ```
  
  (this maybe screams for `get_or_create()`, we'll feel it out as we develop). It's unclear if it's worth making a distinction between `storage.NOUN.update <--> PUT` and `storage.NOUN.patch <--> PATCH`. (As of right now, we don't implement `PUT` / `update` anywhere.)
-   [x] `exists()` should use `fields` in the requests to minimize the payload.
-   [ ] A `Connection` should not be required to be bound to any object (one of the nouns `Bucket`, `ACL`, or `Blob`) but should be an optional argument to methods which actually talk to the API.
-   [ ] We should **strongly** consider adding a `last_updated` field to classes to indicate the last time the values were updated (from the server).
-   [ ] For list methods: List all buckets top-level, e.g.
  
  ``` python
  storage.get_all_buckets(connection=optional_connection)
  ```
  
  and then `bucket.get_all_objects()`. It's unclear how the other 3 nouns (`objectAccessControls`, `bucketAccessControls` and `defaultObjectAccessControls`) will handle this. Right now they are handled via `ObjectACL.reload()` and `BucketACL.reload()` (a superclass of `DefaultObjectACL`).
-   [x] Implicit behavior (default project, default bucket and/or default connection) should be used wherever possible (and documented)

@tseaver Please weigh in. This was inspired by our [discussion](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/pull/604#issuecomment-73929760) at the end of #604.

/cc @thobrla I'd like to close #545 and focus on this (possibly broken down into sub-bugs). Does that sound OK?

issue_title: TimeSeries Query for last 5 Minutes not Last 5 Minutes
issue_body:
The [usage docs](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/docs/monitoring-usage.rst) say 

    query = client.query(METRIC, minutes=5)

will give you stats for the last five minutes. But since [the code](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/monitoring/google/cloud/monitoring/query.py#L128) replaces seconds and microseconds with 0, it's not actually the last 5 minutes since you won't be querying any time interval between the end of the previous minute and the current second.

The reason this came up is in the system tests, I write a point, then try to query it back, but I never get the time series even if they are there, since I 

write_point: 2016-09-28T19:29:57.726637
query end_time:  2016-09-28T19:29:00.000000Z

so even though `write_point` happened first, it's end_time is after the end_time of the query.

Maybe people think second granularity doesn't matter? I'm curious why replacing the second/microseconds with 0 was done in the first place. I think at a minimum the docs are slightly misleading, since it's not the last 5 minutes in the colloquial sense (doesn't include the seconds up until now in the current minute).

cc @rimey @supriyagarg

issue_title: Dependency resolver bug
issue_body:
I apologize -- I don't have a minimal reproducible test case for this.

I have a repo with 3rdparty/python/requirements.txt
In our pants.ini we point to both our internal pypi repo with our custom modules, and a mirror of the public pypi repo.

When I ran all the tests I got
```
Exception message: Could not satisfy all requirements for oauthlib>=0.6.2:
    oauthlib>=0.6.2(from: Flask-OAuthlib==0.9.3), oauthlib==0.6.3(from: auth-client==0.2.3)
```

Which doesn't make any sense, because oauthlib>=0.6.2 and oauthlib==0.6.3 should be perfectly compatible. Flask-OAuthlib is something we're getting from our pypi mirror, whereas auth-client is from our internal custom pypi repo. oauthlib is from the pypi mirror.

I found the package that depended on auth-client, and explicitly added 3rdparty/python:oauthlib to the dependencies list in the BUILD file as suggested by @kwlzn, and this error was fixed.

issue_title: dockerize CI
issue_body:
we should consider wrapping all CI execution with docker. because travis directly supports docker, I believe this should be as simple as constructing/selecting an image and prefixing all invocations of e.g. `ci.sh` in travis.yml with docker calls.

the benefits, afaict would be:

- codified image builds (via Dockerfile) - which improves clarity on the image setup/contents and permits delta modification.
- a consistent unified environment for both CI execution as well as engine binary builds.
- isolation from Travis image changes.
- a locally addressable image for repros and debugging (i.e. you can run ci.sh or specific tests that are failing in CI in the exact same environment, locally).

my vote would be to target CentOS7 for the base image, but I'm obv a little biased.
