issue_title: for non-test-related pytest failures, pants still tries to parse junit.xml
issue_body:
On Pants v1.3.0, in `src/python/pants/backend/python/tasks2/pytest_run.py`, the `_run_pytest` method will invoke the `_get_failed_targets_from_junitxml` method regardless of whether pex got far enough to even invoke tests. This results in an irrelevant error message that pants had an "Error parsing xml file" when in fact the subprocess invocation never got that far to even contemplate generating the junit XML output.

For example, if pex fails to find pytest, then pytest will never be invoked. The junit XML output won't exist in this case.

issue_title: Support for matching directories with PathGlobs
issue_body:
Currently Paths _mostly_ represent "existing files or directories", and they are becoming a native type for the purposes of invalidation. There is one case where they are used (by dependency inference) to represent "possibly existing directories" (Scala dep inference). Rather than Paths, that usecase should likely be using PathGlobs to match the directories instead.

In order to become a native type though, we need to provide some other way to trigger the lookup of paths.... and I think that that is probably allowing PathGlobs to match directories.

issue_title: Nodejs distribution doesn't get installed when yarnpkg is configured as package manager
issue_body:
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/contrib/node/src/python/pants/contrib/node/subsystems/node_distribution.py#L100
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/contrib/node/src/python/pants/contrib/node/subsystems/node_distribution.py#L218

The node binary doesn't get installed when the NodeDistribution class is being constructed. The nodejs binary is installed on the fly when the binary path is being used. This bug didn't appear when there are only two commands (node,npm) in node distribution. No matter which command gets invoked, pants will try to install both binaries within a single package. However, this assumption has changed when yarnpkg is contained in a separate package. Whenever the yarnpkg invoked, the node distribution is required but the path for node distribution is not required. It makes yarnpkg trying to find node distribution from the environment instead of pants binaries. So the node distribution installation should always explicit run when NodeDistribution class is constructed.

issue_title: BinaryUtil ignores fetch timeout

It defaults to 30 seconds. However, this timeout is [not passed to `fetcher.download`](https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/44be4da8bda29e21061e550b6529a346934b9b0f/src/python/pants/binaries/binary_util.py#L158) and therefore ignored.

issue_title: Channels that are deleted once remain 'deleted' as Model
issue_body:
If, in a single session, you delete a Channel from the Content Management Page, that Channel and the Model associated with that GUID (which is static) is flagged as 'deleted' for the remainder of the session.

One consequence is that if you delete a channel on the content management UI, and then try to re-import it, it will not appear on the channel list after dismissing the 'success' notification.

## Steps
0. Delete all channels and restart server
1. Import channel(s) via any method
2. Delete those channels
3. Re-import the channels via any method
4. On dismissing success notification, expect to see channels appear below, but see no channels.
5. Channels will reappear if one refreshes browser.

## Analysis

The reason for this is that after the first time deleting the channels, the ChannelResource Model associated with that id is flagged as 'deleted'. 

So the next time `Collection.getCollection().fetch()` is called, it returns `Collection.data`, which filters on that `deleted` flag (i.e. the previously deleted, but re-imported Channels are filtered out).

(From log at http://github.com/jonboiser/kolibri/blob/ea1931c325926e3ff089e71791764eedb2b3a571/kolibri/core/assets/src/api-resource.js#L382)
**Before Delete**

**After Delete**
![screen shot 2017-09-06 at 12 42 14 pm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/10248067/30131271-0286e8d4-9301-11e7-8477-aed491a19a08.png)

issue_title: Collect static does not seem to get run even when a new version is being run
issue_body:
## Summary

It seems like the following version checking: https://github.com/learningequality/kolibri/blob/develop/kolibri/utils/cli.py#L143 is not triggering an update, even when a newer versioned pex file is being used. I am assuming this is because the VERSION file and the kolibri.__version__ are actually reporting the same version number, and hence the update is not getting triggered.

## System information

 - Version: Develop Branch
 - Operating system: Android
 - Browser: Android Web View

## Real-life consequences (anything community should be aware of, for instance how it affects your deployment)

Any Kolibri updates will not trigger updated front end assets.

issue_title: Versioning Kolibri
issue_body:
Help ensure that standalone plugins are built/used with the correct version of Kolibri.

Static JS assets are now built into separate bundles such that plugins can use different versions of NPM dependencies (right @rtibbles ?)

So, the remaining piece of the puzzle is to be able to specify the versions of the Kolibri core which a plugin is dependent on. This should be enforced at build time but possibly also feed into the `setup.py` metadata such that the plugin isn't installed with the wrong version of Kolibri.

Finally, at Kolibri load-time, the plugin should fail to load if the wrong version of Kolibri is calling it.

We can find inspiration possibly by how Firefox and Thunderbird extensions depend on their host systems.

issue_body:
Minor issue with no user-facing impact. Some apps use URLs that look like:

```
/coach/#/6e54aec31b1a5672ec663c5e11b354c3/groups
```

Others use URLs that look like:

```
/user#/profile
```

Note that there is no `/` between `user` and `#`. This is also the case for management app URLs - both `device` and `facility`.

Recommend we standardize on URLs that have a `/` before the `#`.

issue_title: refactor FormData model
issue_body:
We need a better way to store data in the model, to allow us to keep track of field positions in the form and labels, otherwise export becomes very tricky.

### Braindump

Considering the user can move fields around and also change their labels, there's really no 100% accurate way of properly exporting the data, for example:

User creates form with field A and B then let's say 5 people submit the form, after the 5th person, the admin comes and modifies the form to add a new field C, then 5 more people submit the form. So since the new field C was introduced after the first 5 submissions, then the export file will NOT have a value for column C for the first 5 submissions, but it will for the other 5.

Now, since field C is a new field then somehow I need to know it exists and put this new field in the export file header, so to do this I rely on the latest form submitted of the chosen type and then use the fields on that form to build the export file header, I use the latest in order to keep up with user changes. In other words, the user can add / remove as many fields as they want, the export file will only reflect the state of the latest submitted form.

Also, if a field gets moved or changes it's label then there's sadly no way to match that field with it's value on export for forms that were submitted before the field change, this is  because of a flaw on how the form data is stored.

issue_title: Implement repo as valid, recommended, key in layer.yaml
issue_body:
In `layer.yaml` we should strongly recommend that an additional `repo` key which will point to the upstream repository for that layer. When not present, build should WARN that this isn't set. `charm proof` should also WARN when a .build.manifest file is present but a repository isn't listed.

Example values:

- https://github.com/user/layer-repo
- git@github.com:user/layer-repo
- lp:layer-repo
- lp:~user/+junk/layer-repo
- https://git.launchpad.net/layer-repo
- bzr+ssh://bazaar.launchpad.net/layer-repo

All of these should be accessible from their respective tools directly as valid urls

2018-02-02 08:19:32 DEBUG update-status File "/var/lib/juju/agents/unit-neutron-openvswitch-4/charm/hooks/charmhelpers/contrib/openstack/templating.py", line 304, in complete_contexts

2018-02-02 08:19:32 DEBUG update-status File "/var/lib/juju/agents/unit-neutron-openvswitch-4/charm/hooks/charmhelpers/contrib/openstack/templating.py", line 109, in context
2018-02-02 08:19:32 DEBUG update-status _ctxt = context()
2018-02-02 08:19:32 DEBUG update-status File "/var/lib/juju/agents/unit-neutron-openvswitch-4/charm/hooks/charmhelpers/contrib/openstack/context.py", line 981, in __call__

2018-02-02 08:19:32 DEBUG update-status File "/var/lib/juju/agents/unit-neutron-openvswitch-4/charm/hooks/neutron_ovs_context.py", line 108, in ovs_ctxt
2018-02-02 08:19:32 DEBUG update-status fallback)
2018-02-02 08:19:32 DEBUG update-status File "/var/lib/juju/agents/unit-neutron-openvswitch-4/charm/hooks/charmhelpers/contrib/network/ip.py", line 112, in get_address_in_network
2018-02-02 08:19:32 DEBUG update-status addresses = netifaces.ifaddresses(iface)
2018-02-02 08:19:32 DEBUG update-status ValueError: You must specify a valid interface name.
2018-02-02 08:19:32 ERROR juju.worker.uniter.operation runhook.go:107 hook "update-status" failed: exit status 1

```

As the stack trace says, it happens in ip.py", line 112:
```
        for iface in netifaces.interfaces():
            addresses = netifaces.ifaddresses(iface)
```

By running such a loop manually, I could see that it fails 50% of the time, either on a tap device or on a qbr* one:
```
tapc3c1d498-70
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./debug_netifaces.py", line 9, in <module>
    print(netifaces.ifaddresses(iface))
ValueError: You must specify a valid interface name.
```

My understanding is that this is a race condition, if an instance is present when netifaces.interfaces() but has been deleted when the loop reaches its netifaces.ifaddresses(iface). (or just if a port was deleted)
At the moment, netifaces.interfaces() returns 198 interfaces.

This results in the neutron-openvswitch unit being in error 50% of the time.

issue_title: FIB Manipulation
issue_body:
Hello! I am planning on adding FIB manipulation functionality to exaBGP. Any input from the community is appreciated :)

Here are some of my thoughts so far: 

Linux itself has a FIB where we can store the source, destination, next-hop IP's and interface. What we need to create is a BGP-table in addition to that, where we store all of the BGP routes and their metrics. Then a function determines which routes from the BGP-table are the optimal routes and injects those into the FIB table.

There are two libraries I've found to choose from: [IPDB and IPRoute](http://docs.pyroute2.org/ipdb.html#ipdb-vs-iproute). IPDB starts several implicit threads, and keeps a cache of pulled objects, making it better for applications that request lots of objects from the network device. IPRoute is an alternative that essentially does the same things as IPDB, just without starting new threads, and it grabs the whole network object every single time instead of keeping a cache. 

I am leaning towards IPDB for the efficiency gain, but Thomas pointed out that multi-threading could lead to some harder to debug issues.

Those are my thoughts so far, I'm sure I'll have a lot more as I get started coding. 

Thanks,
Lisa

issue_title: Publish service port in host mode
issue_body:
Hello,

I'm trying to create a service publishing a port in host mode with the Python SDK, but i don't see option to do so :(

It is possible via docker command line (https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/service_create/#options) : 
`docker service create -p mode=host,published=80,target=80 --name test httpd`

It is also possible via docker-compose:
````
services:
  test:
    image: httpd
    ports:
      - mode: host
        target: 80
        published: 80
    deploy:
      mode: replicated
      replicas: 1
````

Is there a way to achieve the same result via python SDK ?

Thanks !

issue_title: Hardening for URL request construction
issue_body:
The `Client` class performs a lot URL construction like this:

        res = self._get(self._url("/exec/{0}/json".format(exec_id)))

This can be used to manipulate the query string if `exec_id` contains characters like `'?'` and `'/'`, which can lead to vulnerabilities elsewhere.  The client code should be fixed to reject such invalid parameters.

Downstream bug report: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1248031

issue_title: Wrong documentation of the parameter "networks" of containers.run() and containers.create()
issue_body: The documentation says that the parameter "networks" is supported and a list of network names should be passed. But the parameter is called "network" and only a single network can be passed to both commands. An update of the documentation or the commands is needed.

issue_title: Handling docker permissions: using a subprocess
issue_body:
So currently the user running this library has to have or root permissions or be in the docker group (which is equivalent to root). For daily development use (I use this library to prepare environments for unit testing) both of these approaches seems dangerous to do on a regular basis.

I would suggest that this library spawns a subprocess which then asks for escaled privileges to root. And then library communicates with that process only. At the time of escalation user could be asked for the root or sudo password. This would allow user to control much better when a regular user (and this library) can access Docker.

A similar issue happens if one is using virtualenv. If you just develop using virtualenv as a regular user and then want to run a script which has to access Docker, but you do not want to have this regular user always permissions of docker group, you can use sudo. But this then breaks out of the virtualenv when executed.

issue_title: assert_rst_strings_are_equal() can result false positives
issue_body:
The function completely ignores blank lines.
In reality, in an RST string a single blank line is equal to 42 contiguous blank lines. But it's not the same as zero blank lines.

How to fix:

a) splitlines
b) collapse multiple contiguous blank lines into one
c) assert len() is equal
d) strip trailing whitespace in each element
e) assert each line is equal

This improves further on #448

issue_title: Terminology: "Component" vs "Session"
issue_body:
In almost all examples, and in much of the documentation, the term "session" and "component" are mixed in a slightly confusing way.

E.g. many of the examples: `class Component(ApplicationSession):`. This just rhymes bad to me. Why not call the derived class `Session`? It derives from an Autobahn `ApplicationSession`.

I guess you could argue that you build your "component", and that "component" happen to be implemented as an Autobahn `ApplicationSession`. But I think naming things are important and would really like to hear why the terms "session" and "component" are mixed like this in the code.

I'm itching to call my own such classes `FooSession` and `BarSession`, to follow the naming of the superclass... but then in the API there's `autobahn.wamp.types.ComponentConfig`... Again, why is this called "component"? Isn't it a configuration for an application session? In fact, this is the only class I can find in the public API which is named something with "component". I would expect this class to be called either `ApplicationSessionConfig` or just `SessionConfig` (if `ApplicationSession` is to keep its name).

issue_title: AttributeError: 'tuple' object has no attribute 'append'
issue_body:
Relevant part of the stack trace:
```
   chord = MeasureParser().xmlToChordSymbol(xml_element)
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.5/site-packages/music21/musicxml/xmlToM21.py", line 3336, in xmlToChordSymbol
    cs._updatePitches()
  File "/app/.heroku/python/lib/python3.5/site-packages/music21/harmony.py", line 1899, in _updatePitches
    pitches.append(self._bass)
AttributeError: 'tuple' object has no attribute 'append'
```

In file `harmony.py`, there are lines:
```python
        pitches = self._adjustOctaves(pitches)

        if self._root.name != self._bass.name:

            inversionNum = self.inversion()
        
            if not self.inversionIsValid(inversionNum):
                #there is a bass, yet no normal inversion was found....must be added note 
            
                inversionNum = None
                self._bass.octave = 2 # arbitrary octave, must be below root, 
                                    # which was arbitrarily chosen as 3 above
                pitches.append(self._bass)
```

The problem here is that `_adjustOctaves` returns a tuple, so calling `pitches.append` is incorrect. I guess `_adjustOctaves` should just return a list - I don't think it will break anything because the method is only used in one place.

https://github.com/soft-matter/trackpy/wiki/The-Ten-Commandments-for-configuring-trackpy-on-Anaconda-on-Debian-Linux-on-Windows-10-using-WSL

If you install pims or trackpy inside a user-created conda environment rather than in the root environment, then expect errors as I document below. After many attempts to get this right LOL..... my Wiki instructions **should** now work perfectly and be error-free (2018-03-17, trackpy 0.3.3, python 3.6).

UPDATE: OH DAMMIT, THIS STILL FAILS. SO THIS APPEARS TO BE HOW IT WORKS....

CASE 1: INSTALL PIMS AND TRACKPY IN THE ROOT ENV ---> CANNOT IMPORT PIMS OR TRACKPY FROM THE COMMAND LINE INSIDE A CREATED CONDA ENV.

CASE 2: INSTALL PIMS AND TRACKPY IN A CREATED CONDA ENV ---> CANNOT IMPORT PIMS OR TRACKPY INTO JUPYTER NOTEBOOK.

SIGH.

issue_title: Add possibility to connect more than 1 Crazyflie per program
issue_body:
Currently the Crazyflie lib is built on the assumption that one Python program connects to only one Crazyflie.

If we want to be able to fly swarm of Crazyflies using the lib, we need to be able to connect more than one Crazyflie.

One solution is to be able to create multiple Crazyflie object and to share Crazyradio.

issue_title: Updated CI build naming convention
issue_body:
Change "{.name}-CI-{.version}-{.ref}-{YYYYMMDD_hhmmss}[-{target}].ext" to "{.name}-CI-{.version}-{YYYYMMDD_hhmmss}-{.ref}[-{target}].ext" to ensure sorting based on timestamp, but not on revision.

issue_title: Proposal: disable /router-nginx-status endpoint in production
issue_body:
Currently, the default virtual host on `deis-router` exposes a `/router-nginx-status` endpoint.

If a request is made that hits the router and isn't matched by the virtual host for any specific app, it will be served by the default virtual host.

A cleverly constructed URL like the one below allows visibility into the router from the outside world.

Example:

http://any-subdomain-that-is-not-an-app.krancour.deis.ninja/router-nginx-status

For fear that this information could be exploited, I believe we should add the capability to disable this endpoint in production.  Better yet, IMO, we should disable it by default and allow operators who understand the risk to opt-in.

issue_title: Beacon chain loves Trinity
issue_body:
Recently, research team is working on refining [the new spec](https://notes.ethereum.org/s/Syj3QZSxm) and the [**beacon chain** PoC codebase](https://github.com/ethereum/beacon_chain)  (**big shout out to V!**). Basically, there’s a beacon chain that will produce random numbers with RANDAO-based scheme, and we can build Casper logic in the beacon chain to support both beacon chain and shard chains staking.

This issue is for discussing (1) what feature/components are required and (2) what's the blueprint looks like *if we want to implement the beacon chain in the Trinity pyeth world*?

cc @djrtwo , please correct me if I'm wrong. :)

## The certain differences between the main PoW chain and beacon chain
* They both have *Block*, but different fields.
* They both have *State*, but totally different content.
* The main chain uses EVM; beacon chain doesn’t.
* The main chain is still the PoW chain; beacon chain is the full PoS chain (and can implicitly finalize the main PoW chain).

## The potential differences between the main PoW chain and beacon chain
* Beacon chain may use a different serialization scheme instead of RLP.
* Beacon chain may use a different p2p layer for integrating with shard chain networks.
* The main chain has a one-way burns contract; the Casper logic is implemented in beacon chain protocol.
* Main PoW chain, beacon chain, and shards relationship:
     * **Main PoW chain <> beacon**: To add and verify the `main_chain_ref` in beacon block, **the beacon chain validators have to follow the actual canonical main PoW chain**. (see [Beacon-chain-processing](https://notes.ethereum.org/s/Syj3QZSxm#Beacon-chain-processing for more details)
     * **beacon <> shards**: One active validators queue for the validators serve different roles; the validator could be sampled as beacon/shards proposer, notary, attester... etc., so they will have to be both beacon client and shards client.

## Discussion
1. Given those differences, it probably makes sense to have a `beacon` module besides `evm` to put the chain logic?
2. If (1), since there’s no EVM in beacon chain, it will be much simpler here. How many components do we need?
	1. Start by mirroring the reusable `evm` architecture.
        ```
        ├── chains
        ├── constants.py
        ├── db
        ├── exceptions.py
        ├── tools
        ├── utils
        └── validation.py
        ```
	2. Move and reorganize the beacon logics to here.
3. Is there anything we need to be aware of?

issue_title: Package type
issue_body:
We have a problem, which will be amplified quite a bit when the R support gets fleshed out, that there are naming conflicts between different packages from different languages. We already see this a little bit with `lxml`.  I'll use `bokeh` as an example, because we are about to have both Python and R bindings for it.

The solution I've been using so far with R is to prefix all R package names with `r-`, but @kriehl pointed out that many R users aren't going to like this, or will be confused by it. 

My idea (and I'm not sure how this would be implemented yet) would be for each package to optionally have an associated "type". Two packages with the same name can coexist, if they have different types. Whenever a package name is used ambiguously on the command line, conda would ask which type you meant. You could also specify the type using some syntax (like say `r:bokeh`). You could also specify some configuration value to always prefer some given type (like say if you only use Python and don't care about R).

Note that this shouldn't be confused with https://github.com/conda/conda-build/issues/155, which is a completely different feature.

The issue here is that Astropy is still the one built against Numpy 1.9, so when using some modules which were compiled against the Numpy C API, one gets warnings along the lines of:

```
module compiled against API version a but this version of numpy is 9
```

TL;DR: if updating Numpy, packages that depend on a specific version of Numpy should be updated too.

issue_title: Proxy configuration per URL
issue_body:
In a complex network with multiple proxies, requests need to be routed to different proxies that depend on the URL.  I propose that we extend the proxy_servers key in the conda config to support arbitrary prefixes, rather than just 'http' and 'https' keys, and that the longest matching key should be used.

For example, the proxy_servers config could be:

```yaml
proxy_servers:
    http: http://some.proxy.com:8080
    https: https://some.proxy.com:8081
    'http://some.internal.url': http://internal.proxy.com
    'http://some.external.url': http://external.proxy.com
```
Any request is matched to the longest string, and that given proxy is used.

A weakness of this proposal is that often you want to match the end of a domain name in proxy configurations, i.e., any domain that ends in 'mycompany.com' should use a specific proxy.  However, for the specific purposes of conda, perhaps it's all right to hardcode the specific url conda would use, like 'http://mypackages.mycompany.com'.

This addresses, in a way, the problems in #190.

issue_title: conda fix permissions
issue_body:
I want a new command in conda 4.2 that is something like `conda fix --permissions`, which simply subprocesses `sudo chown -R $USER:$USER {{ config.root_dir }}` on unix, and has an equivalent Windows operation.

Our issue backlog is littered with permissions errors that are easily fixed.  Like this one: https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/2667

CC @msarahan @mingwandroid

issue_title: conda remove while offline fails with stack trace
issue_body:
I am trying to remove a bunch of R packages (in fact, all of them).  I am now offline in an airport (OK, I was when the problem occurred: I'm now online to report the issue), and the operation fails with a stack trace `HTTPSConnectionPool` exception.

Expected behavior:

* I'm just removing packages: they get removed.

Unexpected behavior:

* I'm just removing packages, but Conda decides it wants to update `pip`
* Conda would attempt to connect to any remote service when there is no network connection of any sort
* If there is a network error (a very common sort of problem for package installers) that Conda would report that error via a stack trace.

issue_title: Error during package installation ('maximum recursion depth exceeded',)
issue_body:
Hi, **I'm submitting a...** - [x] bug report - [ ] feature request ## Current Behavior On a fresh conda installation, when creating a conda environment with "large" packages like mkl-2018.0.1, which is a dependancy requirement for Numpy, the download of some packages stops quickly, and conda fails with "maximum recursion depth exceeded" errors. Notes: - I am behind an entreprise proxy (for which I don't have much info). But I was using conda since one year without any troubles on that same environment. I discovered the problem around Week 8. I was able to reproduce the problem on other servers behind the same proxy, or on a different proxy (for which I don't details neither) - there is no issue to donwload stuff from internet from that server. I can manually download the faulty packages from https://repo.continuum.io/pkgs/main/linux-64/ repository with curl or wget whithout any troubles. But that fails with conda for certain packages - this seems to concern large packages. The problem now occurs systematically with mkl-2018.0.1 - If the packages to install are already in the local cache, the problem might not be visible - Currently I am testing with latest version of conda package and latest version of Miniconda3 installer. But I have tested also with older versions that proved to be working before for me. Same error. I wonder if this could be similar/related to https://github.com/conda/conda/issues/5032 ? ### Steps to Reproduce ``` # In a CentOS7 docker image or VM curl -o /tmp/Miniconda3-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh         conda create -n _test-env python=2.7 itsdangers conda=X.X

2. Subprocess to that conda, and make sure that conda can operate on that environment.

                CONDA_ROOT/envs/_test-env/bin/conda install -p CONDA_ROOT/envs/_test-env/bin/conda flask

issue_title: conda locking rudimentary and fragile
issue_body:
The conda lock is currently implemented using a two-step `glob()` / `open()` sequence. This has several issues:
* there is an inherent race condition: two processes could satisfy the `glob()` condition and take the lock at the same time
* the lock isn't cleaned up when a process terminates abruptly (which can happen when e.g. a CI job gets killed)

There are various ways to do robust file-based locking. Cross-platform Python libraries exist for this, e.g. [locket](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/locket/) or [py-filelock](https://filelock.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

issue_title: Pinned packages in package structure
issue_body:
Hey guys, 

I have a package A which depends on package B. I build both myself. They both depend on various third-party libraries. 
Now I would like to be able to pin for example `pandas` to version `x`. I the following error: 
Error: Unsatisfiable package specifications in various cases: 
1) Pin in package A to `x` and no pin in package B
2) Pin in package A to `x` and pin in package B to `x`. 

I could understand that in 1), "no pin in B" can be interpreted as newest version and therefore constitute a conflict. But 2) seems puzzling to me. 

Am I overlooking something here?

issue_title: Syntax highlighting: free format sets all lines starting with * as comment lines
issue_body:
Example:

~~~cobol
        compute a = a
                  * b
                  / 20
~~~

Free format has only inline comments, these start with `*>`.
There are other formats like the ACU terminal format (where comments start in col 1 with an asterisk, debugging lines on col 1 with `D`, ...) but as GnuCOBOL doesn't support it (yet) we may don't need to support this in OCIDE.
Or add a checkbox "asterisk in column 1 as inline comment" (MF has a similar setting which GnuCOBOL supports) in Preferences... In any case this would only be about col 1.

issue_title: What happens by [SHIFT]+[POS1]?
issue_body:
In fixed-form reference-format:

1. [POS1] sets the cursor position to the first character written (in the code area) when the current cursor is to the right of this position.
2. [POS1] sets the cursor position to column 8 (start of area a) when the current cursor is not to the right of the position mentioned under 1.
3. [POS1] should ideally place the cursor in column 1 if it is currently at position 8 or before - it doesn't.

I've "assumed" that [SHIFT]+[POS1] will directly position the cursor in column 1 but it does "weird stuff" instead... What is it supposed to do?

Can we get [SHIFT]+[POS1] jump to col1 and [POS1] at or before column 1 to jump to col1, too?

issue_title: Reindex on ElasticSearch fails but Curator continues to execute script
issue_body:
Curator performs reindex and ES fails, but Curator continues execution.

### Expected Behavior
Curator should break process and exit with error when error occurs during reindex operation.

### Actual Behavior
Curator continues processing, executing next steps.

### Steps to Reproduce the Problem
1. Create test index:
```
actions:
  1:
    action: create_index
    description: Create the test index
    options:
      name: test_index
      extra_settings:
        settings:
          number_of_shards: 5
          number_of_replicas: 1
        mappings:
          testindexmodel:
            properties:
              testId:
                type: keyword
              price:
                index: false
                type: float
      disable_action: False
```
2. Load data
```
PUT test_index/testindexmodel/1
{
    "testId" : "1",
    "price" : 123.4
}

PUT test_index/testindexmodel/2
{
    "testId" : "2",
    "price" : 123.4,
    "count" : 223
}
PUT test_index/testindexmodel/3
{
  "testId": "3",
  "price": 245.7
}
```
3. Execute upgrade script
```
actions:
  1:
    action: create_index
    description: Create the test index
    options:
      name: test_index_new
      extra_settings:
        settings:
          number_of_shards: 5
          number_of_replicas: 1
        mappings:
          testindexmodel:
            properties:
              testId:
                type: keyword
              price:
                index: false
                type: float
              count:
                index: false
                type: byte
      disable_action: False
  2:
    description: Copy data to the new index
    action: reindex
    options:
      disable_action: False
      continue_if_exception: False
      wait_interval: 9
      max_wait: -1
      request_body:
        source:
          index: test_index
        dest:
          index: test_index_new
    filters:
    - filtertype: none
  3:
    action: delete_indices
    description: Delete obsolete index
    options:
      timeout_override: 300
      continue_if_exception: False
    filters:
      - filtertype: pattern
        kind: regex
        value: '^test_index$'
```
4. Validate documents count - one should be missing.

### Specifications

  - Version: curator-5.5.4-amd64, ElasticSearch: 6.2.2
  - Platform: Curator was run on Windows 10 Pro, 10.0.17134, ES is deployed on Debian 8


## Context (Environment)
Trying to update index with new fields.

## Detailed Description
I have an index with specified structure and filled with about 300k documents. During development, index was filled with about 10 documents that had one property not presented in index mapping (it had values like 150, 225, 250).
Then I created script that "officially" added that property (of byte type) into mapping. Script created new index with new mapping, copied documents and deleted old index (thats a simplified scenario, but it does not matter here). Script executed successfully, but then I noticed that new index lacks a lot of documents.
When I rerun that script with DEBUG logging level I spotted that during reindex error occured, but Curator continued with next steps. Error details:

issue_title: Allow upload of VHD during `vm create`
[Test] ScenarioTest doesn't work with VM create
issue_body:
In it's current form, the new ScenarioTest test base can't be used to record tests that use VM create, which limits widespread implementation and conversion of existing tests. The old test base (VCRTestBase) had logic that handled the deployment name parameter to allow the recordings to be played back. The new base doesn't have that and thus the recordings cannot be replayed.

Once this works for any single VM create test scenario, it should work for them all.

There is no way to set the provider namespace for the `az feature` sub-commands. The namespace `Microsoft.Features` is always used.

**To Reproduce**

Command

Arguments

Once the feature 'AllowMultipleIpConfigurationsPerNic' is registered, invoking 'az provider register -n Microsoft.Network' is required to get the change propagated
The resource namespace 'Microsoft.Features' is invalid.

issue_title: PUBLIC NOTICE: Repository and packages changes

You may have noticed that we've been working on splitting the Azure package into several smaller packages.  You will be able to `pip install azure` and get everything, but you'll also be able to just install the parts you need `pip install azure-servicebus`.

So far this work was done in the dev branch, but I've just merged those changes into master.  I will be working on updating readmes, merging pull requests, etc.  We're aiming to do a release to PyPI (pre-release) end of this week or early next week.

As part of this reorganization, the Azure storage team is taking ownership of the storage runtime library.  We've moved the code to a new repository: https://github.com/Azure/azure-storage-python.  I'll be removing the copy we have in this repository shortly.

You'll notice new storage features soon, the first one being support for File Service.  We'll be porting the storage issues over there and take care of the storage pull requests.

I'll update this with the latest status during the week.

issue_title: Parallel execution of individual hooks?
issue_body:
I have this one hook that runs `puppet parser validate` which is extremely slow. Running it on just 185 files takes 105 seconds which makes my tests (which call `pre-commit run --all-files`) annoying enough to run that most people don't run them locally.

What if pre-commit's xargs implementation could do things in parallel? Here's a sketch of a patch that does that (only works on Python 3): https://i.fluffy.cc/t43V5vqd3VH9lTQfl8djnfZWBV2zDDTZ.html

This takes my test time from 105 seconds to 15 seconds.

Some thoughts:

* If any hooks write to files besides the ones they are linting, this could break. This is a problem, though pre-commit is heavily designed around operations on individual files, so the vast majority of hooks should be okay. We could offer an opt-in or opt-out at the individual hook level?
* Parallelizing different hooks (running puppet-lint at the same time as puppet-validate) would be neat but I think is way more problematic (and introduces crazy locking problems since you can't have two hooks running on the same file at once).
* Because pre-commit captures output and displays it at the end, I don't think we have any of the usual problems of interleaved/confusing output. The printing happens in the main thread when collecting statuses and shouldn't have races.
* `concurrent.futures` works great here, but is Python 3 only. I'm not sure how hard this is to do in a compatible way.

@asottile what do you think? Is this too complicated to be worth pursuing?

issue_title: [docs] Add clarification regarding order of middleware execution
issue_body:
## Long story short

Clarify/Explain the order of execution when multiple middleware factories are registered.

## Expected behaviour

Consider the following application:

```
from aiohttp import web
def test(request):
    print('Handler function called')
    return web.Response(text="Hello")

async def middleware1(app, handler):
    async def middleware_handler(request):
        print('Middleware 1 called')
        response = await handler(request)
        print('Middleware 1 finished')

        return response
    return middleware_handler

async def middleware2(app, handler):
    async def middleware_handler(request):
        print('Middleware 2 called')
        response = await handler(request)
        print('Middleware 2 finished')

        return response
    return middleware_handler


app = web.Application(middlewares=[middleware1, middleware2])
app.router.add_get('/', test)
web.run_app(app)
```

The above code would produce the following output:

```
Middleware 1 called
Middleware 2 called
Handler function called
Middleware 2 finished
Middleware 1 finished
```

I think an explanation like this may improve the [current documentation](http://aiohttp.readthedocs.io/en/stable/web.html#middlewares) which is brief.


## Actual behaviour

IMHO, the current explanation linked above is brief and perhaps is not clear to someone new to `aiohttp`.


## Steps to reproduce

NA

## Your environment

NA

issue_title: Make a template filter for loading icons
issue_body:
This should be tied into a map configured in ``settings.py``. So we should be able to do something like this to replace the default font-awesome icons with iconic:
```python
DJANGO_CRADMIN_CSS_ICON_MAP = {
    'search': 'iconic iconic-eye-open',
    'x': 'iconic iconic-x',
   # ...
}
```

## The tag will work like this:
```htmldjango
{% load cradmin_icon_tags %}
<span class="{% cradmin_icon 'search' %}"><span>
```

## Easy to configure and extend
We should also be able to extend an existing icon set with:
```python
from django_cradmin import css_icon_map
DJANGO_CRADMIN_CSS_ICON_MAP = css_icon_map.FONT_AWESOME.copy()
DJANGO_CRADMIN_CSS_ICON_MAP.update({
    'search': 'my my-search-icon'
})
```

## Included icons and maps
We should include font-awesome as a dependency, but not as part of the LESS. Instead it should be included in a separate block in the base template, and the path should be configurable through the ``DJANGO_CRADMIN_CSS_ICON_LIBRARY_PATH`` setting.

We should also include maps for other icon sets. To begin with, we will also include a map for Iconic.

issue_title: Add 'description' to fields and models
issue_body:
Descriptions are a fundamental aspect of a well-designed schema.  They are not only great documentation for other developers, they can be programmatically inspected to populate UIs, etc. They're a key part of the JSON Schema specification and so their addition to `schematics` would enable tools like [jsonschematics](https://github.com/voidfiles/jsonschematics) to create more compliant output, which would in turn make it more compatible with other tools that take JSON Schema documents as an input.

For types, the description makes sense as a new argument to `BaseType.__init__`.  For `Model`s, the value could be taken from the object's `__doc__` attribute (or perhaps just the first sentence).

Thanks for listening.

issue_title: Add rule to exclude a path, like the inverse of `pick`
issue_body:
Feature request: In cases where there are lots of directories and files to include from a dependency module _except one_, it would be nice to be able to specify explicit paths to omit completely, rather than using `pick` to verbosely declare everything to include.

issue_title: [FYI] - Android Studio allows to generate invalid x509 certificates
issue_body:
Hi!
Android Studio allows to generate invalid x509 certificates. The form allows you to specify a country name on any number of chars. RFC specifies that the country name must be a 2 character country code.

Androguard uses the cryptography library to parse x509 certificates which had signed the APK. If the country name of the certificate is invalid, the cryptography library raises this error:
```
ssl/x509.py", line 102, in issuer
    return _decode_x509_name(self._backend, issuer)
  File "[edited]/exodus-standalone/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/cryptography/hazmat/backends/openssl/decode_asn1.py", line 66, in _decode_x509_name
    attribute = _decode_x509_name_entry(backend, entry)
  File "[edited]/exodus-standalone/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/cryptography/hazmat/backends/openssl/decode_asn1.py", line 57, in _decode_x509_name_entry
    return x509.NameAttribute(x509.ObjectIdentifier(oid), value, type)
  File "[edited]/exodus-standalone/venv/lib/python3.5/site-packages/cryptography/x509/name.py", line 50, in __init__
    "Country name must be a 2 character country code"
ValueError: Country name must be a 2 character country code
```

Plus, it seems that Androguard does not handle the [APK Signature Scheme v2 ](https://source.android.com/security/apksigning/v2).

issue_title: [Announcement][Discussion] .NET Core Supported OS Lifecycle Policy
issue_body:
# .NET Core Supported OS Lifecycle Policy

## Summary

.NET Core is supported on a range of operating systems and versions. Each platform has distinct Lifecycles defined by the parent organization. .NET Core support will take these Lifecycle schedules into account when adding or removing versions from the supported list.

## Details

When an OS which is part of the .NET Core supported set releases an update, support for that version will generally be available with the next update to .NET Core. For example Fedora 26 is available and will be supported by .NET Core 2.0. Fedora 27 is scheduled to release in the fall and will be supported by a subsequent update of .NET Core.

'Support' means that .NET Core is built and tested on the OS and Microsoft Developer Support may be contacted for assistance with .NET Core on the environment.

* [.NET Core 2.0 supported OS versions](https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/release-notes/2.0/2.0-supported-os.md)
* [.NET Core 1.x supported OS versions](https://github.com/dotnet/core/blob/master/release-notes/1.0/1.0-supported-os.md)

issue_title: letsencrypt.log should not be truncated with each run
issue_body: Right now, each run of letsencrypt truncates the existing letsencrypt.log. Instead we should always append to letsencrypt.log. That way on systems that run letsencrypt repeatedly in an automated way, errors from earlier invocations are not obliterated by later invocations.

issue_title: letsencrypt doesn't build on debian stable: AttributeError: 'X509Req' object has no attribute 'get_extension_count'
issue_body:
Moving this here from https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt-debian/issues/6

When I try to build letsencrypt on Debian, I get the following error:

    ERROR: test_csr_no_sans (acme.crypto_util_test.PyOpenSSLCertOrReqSANTest)
    ----------------------------------------------------------------------
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/directory/letsencrypt-debian-master/acme/acme/crypto_util_test.py", line 88, in test_csr_no_sans
        self.assertEqual(self._call_csr('csr-nosans.pem'), [])
      File "/directory/letsencrypt-debian-master/acme/acme/crypto_util_test.py", line 78, in _call_csr
        return self._call(test_util.load_csr, name)
      File "/directory/letsencrypt-debian-master/acme/acme/crypto_util_test.py", line 72, in _call
        return _pyopenssl_cert_or_req_san(loader(name))
      File "/directory/letsencrypt-debian-master/acme/acme/crypto_util.py", line 141, in _pyopenssl_cert_or_req_san
        for i in range(cert_or_req.get_extension_count())]
      File "/directory/letsencrypt-debian-master/acme/acme/jose/util.py", line 44, in __getattr__
        return getattr(self._wrapped, name)
    AttributeError: 'X509Req' object has no attribute 'get_extension_count'

It seems to me that the cause of this issue is in file acme/acme/crypto_util.py, lines 137 to 141:

    if hasattr(cert_or_req, 'get_extensions'):  # X509Req
            extensions = cert_or_req.get_extensions()
        else:  # X509
            extensions = [cert_or_req.get_extension(i)
                          for i in range(cert_or_req.get_extension_count())]

This means that an X509Req object is supposed to have a get_extensions() method while an X509 object is supposed to have two methods called get_extension() and get_extension_count().

However, as far as I can tell, an X509Req object is not supposed to provide any of the aforementioned methods. This means that the if-condition fails on X509Req objects (which doesn't seem to be what we want) and get_extension_count() is called on the X509Req object which causes the compiler error.

Gotcha: The missing get_extensions() method was added in python-openssl v0.15 which is not in Debian Stable (jessie).

Is there a way to make this work with python-openssl v0.14? [or maybe even earlier?]

If the answer is no, we would have to require v0.15 of python-openssl as a build-dependency in debian/control.

issue_title: pack file almost seven times larger than the source code

I would suggest that the maintainers at least look into the possibility of pruning the history to create a more reasonable sized repository.  If this is considered a reasonable request (there may be good reasons why it is not), a google search on 'git pack file large' should offer some avenues to explore.

issue_title: Handling external and internal dependencies
issue_body:
Folium generates a web page that depends on several objects (css,js,json, data...). These objects may be :
* provided by an external web page or web service
* stored on the disk
* embeded into the HTML file

I wonder how folium shall handle that dependencies. Depending on what you need, on how you serve your web page, etc, you may be willing to have everything embeded into a single html, or to have things loaded on the fly.
It becomes tricky when trying to implement plugins that depends on libraries that are only served on HTTP (or only HTTPS).

One could:
* Have every dependency downloaded when installing Folium
* Have everything downloaded when generating the map
* Have everything linked into the html ; so that they are downloaded by the client directly
* Provide a choice between the former strategies

issue_title: tmux: powerline doesn't integrate because of removed configuration opition
issue_body:
When I start tmux I get the basic green status bar, even with the most minimal configuration possible:

```conf
source /usr/lib64/python3.5/site-packages/powerline/bindings/tmux/powerline.conf
```

When I run powerline-config tmux setup I get the following error:
```
unknown option: status-utf8
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...
subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '['tmux', 'set', '-g', 'status-utf8', 'on']' returned non-zero exit status 1
```

The traceback itself isn't very useful, but the stderr output of tmux is, looking at the tmux documentation, seting "status-utf8" has been unnecessary since the 1.0 release, it's auto-detected. When this was filed as a bug against tmux the advice was to remove the option (https://github.com/tmux/tmux/issues/230#), and I can confirm that for me (with tmux 2.2) removing that options from powerline-base.conf indeed fixes the issue and tmux+powerline works as expected.

I don't know how you want to handle this. I'd be happy to send a pull request to remove the configuration option, the documentation seems to suggest that it is safe to do so and it does fix my system with tmux 2.2, where it seems the option is not just deprecated by removed.

issue_title: Field serialize default arguments
issue_body:
Currently [Field.serialize()](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/blob/dev/marshmallow/fields.py#L233) takes self and 2 arguments (attr and obj).

However in basic use cases, attr is not needed.

- [Nested](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/blob/dev/marshmallow/fields.py#L427)
- [List](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/blob/dev/marshmallow/fields.py#L547) -> depends on self.container
- [String](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/blob/dev/marshmallow/fields.py#L582)
- etc.

so I think the `attr` argument of `Field.serialize()` should be optional.

my current workaround is
```python
mybfield = Boolean()
wire_format = mybfield.serialize(0, [True])
assert mybfield.deserialize(wire_format)
```
but this would be nicer : 
```python
mybfield = Boolean()
wire_format = mybfield.serialize(True)
assert mybfield.deserialize(wire_format)
```

Also in term of API design this should make the `deserialize` function the exact inverse of `serialize` function.

issue_title: DateTime deserializer cannot use dateutil
issue_body:
The DateTime deserializer cannot use the dateutil library because it contains an inaccessible code path.

For example, parsing the string `2018/03/26` fails:
```python
>>> import marshmallow as ma
>>> f = ma.fields.DateTime()
>>> f.deserialize('2018/03/26')
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "marshmallow/fields.py", line 261, in deserialize
    output = self._deserialize(value, attr, data)
  File "marshmallow/fields.py", line 929, in _deserialize
    raise self.fail('invalid')
  File "marshmallow/fields.py", line 216, in fail
    raise ValidationError(msg)
marshmallow.exceptions.ValidationError: Not a valid datetime.
```
Even though the `dateutil`-based parser knows how to deserialize it:
```python
>>> ma.utils.dateutil_available
True
>>> ma.utils.from_datestring('2018/03/26')
datetime.datetime(2018, 3, 26, 0, 0)
```

The cause of this is located in [this code block](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/blob/dev/marshmallow/fields.py#L923-L935).  Specifically: `dateutil` is only used when the field does not have a pre-defined date format, but the `deserialize` method itself sets the field's date format to a default value before that condition is evaluated.

I submitted #619 to address this last year, but I admit that the description was poorly worded and I never followed-up with it until recently, when user @znatty22 commented that she has run into the same issue.

Could someone please advise on steps to take in order to get #619 merged?

Thanks,

Kevin

issue_body:

```python

s = Wrapped()

print(s.load({}))

issue_title: fields.Field's missing option with many=True, unexpected behavior
issue_body:
marshmallow's fields.Field has two options.

- default
- missing

I wonder `default` is the hook (or default value) for serialization, and `missing` is the hook for deserialization.

## with many=True option, default is ok

when serialization, default option is correctly, I feel.

```python
from marshmallow import fields, Schema


class S(Schema):
    nums = fields.Integer(many=True, default=[1, 2, 3])

print(S().dump({}).data)
# {'nums': [1, 2, 3]}
```

## with many=true option,  missing is uncomfortable behavior

But,  when deserialization,  current marshmallow's behavior is not good. Maybe, I have misunderstanding for missing option.  Although, using missing option with `many=True`,  treating default value as collection is better, I think.

```python
from marshmallow import fields, Schema


class S(Schema):
    nums = fields.Integer(many=True, missing=[1, 2, 3])

# this is error.
print(S().load({}))
# UnmarshalResult(data={}, errors={'nums': ['Not a valid integer.']})


# if missing's value is 1, error is not raised. but, this is not good, at least me.
class S2(Schema):
    nums = fields.Integer(many=True, missing=1)
print(S2().load({}))
# UnmarshalResult(data={'nums': 1}, errors={})
```

issue_title: Accept plain strings list as Licensing "symbols"
issue_body: The latest code in #6 changes the API and drops the ugly "LicenseRef" object in favor of plain license symbols objects. issue_title: --init-db will hang around the 1000 contract mark
issue_body:
## Overview

Within the past few releases, the `--init-db` command will hang / fail to progress around the ~1000 unique contract mark.  

Expected behavior: Mythril will continue to scan and build the contract database to completion.
Actual Result: Mythril begins to slow before hanging, around ~1000 unique contracts.

## Remarks 

This only seems to happen when generating a new database / from scratch. It also seems to be a recent regression, possibly a deadlock with the recent addition of threading. I was able to build a database prior to the multiprocessing work.

Re-running `--init-db` with an already fully built database does not seem to reproduce this issue. 

I use a local, fully sync'd ETH node (geth 1.8.3).

## Repro

1. Delete any existitng mythril database, eg `rm -rf ~/.mythril/`
2. Run mythril and try to enumerate the database. `myth --init-db --rpc localhost:8545`
3. mythril should hang around the ~1000 unique contract mark. Probably coincidence, but it seems consistent.

Platform: Ubuntu 16.04 server, 4 cores, 8gb RAM + 8gb Swap

issue_title: Support custom reaction on UnsupportedFormat exception
issue_body:
Several issues (#254, #985, #1015) argue around the problem that the UnsupportedFormat exception from the serializer ends up in an HTTP 500 error. This HTTP result code is intended for true server errors, meaning that the server-side implementation has an unexpected problem (== exception), whereas the HTTP request may even be fine. 

My argumentation here is that this is not consistent with the other parts of Tastypie, were the requestor can be clearly blamed for sending something wrong. Since the reaction in such a situation is a developer decision - e.g. I would like to return 415 instead of 500 -, my proposal is to add an official hook for reacting on such problems.

If that would be agreeable, I offer to prepare a patch the simply extends the implementation of the wrapper method (https://github.com/toastdriven/django-tastypie/blob/master/tastypie/resources.py#L192) to call such a hook function. Other ideas are welcome.

issue_title: Energy Production Modeling and Provenance
issue_body:
I think this belongs as its own issue for discussion, but it is closely related to the conversation around #88 and #17.

I am new to PVLIB, contributing to projects on Github, and the scientific python stack, but I do have experience working with other PV energy production modeling software (Helioscope, PVsyst, and SAM).  

One issue that I've encountered when working with these programs is keeping track of revisions to the model, especially when working with a team.  It seems like this issue could be largely resolved by using PVLIB and keeping the file defining the system under version control with git or maybe a more comprehensive provenance system like [Sumatra](https://github.com/open-research/sumatra).

It seems like a good time to express the desire to use PVLIB in this manner because the approach to defining an entire system from module through inverter and step-up XFMR to interconnection like in #88 is still in development.  

I agree with @jforbess point in #88 that CSV input will be useful, but I would like to see a json or yaml convention develop that could be used for version controlled system configuration input files.

issue_title: Segfault when using bcd2 and maxhs maxsat_engines
issue_body:
Z3 (commit 4f5b0667) will generate a segmentation fault when processing the following formula using either the `bcd2` or `maxhs` engines:

````
(set-option :opt.maxsat_engine bcd2)
;(set-option :opt.maxsat_engine maxhs)

(declare-datatypes () ((PartID (P1) (P0))))
(declare-fun n3_p () PartID)
(declare-fun n2_p () PartID)
(declare-fun n1_p () PartID)
(declare-fun n0_p () PartID)
(declare-fun imbalance () Int)
(assert (let ((a!1 (+ 0
              (ite (= n0_p P0) 1 0)
              (ite (= n1_p P0) 1 0)
              (ite (= n2_p P0) 1 0)
              (ite (= n3_p P0) 1 0)))
      (a!2 (+ 0
              (ite (= n0_p P1) 1 0)
              (ite (= n1_p P1) 1 0)
              (ite (= n2_p P1) 1 0)
              (ite (= n3_p P1) 1 0))))
(let ((a!3 (- (ite (and true (>= a!1 a!2)) a!1 a!2)
              (ite (and true (<= a!1 a!2)) a!1 a!2))))
  (= imbalance a!3))))
(assert (<= imbalance 1))
(minimize (+ 0
   (ite (= n0_p n1_p) 0 100)
   (ite (= n0_p n2_p) 0 1)
   (ite (= n1_p n3_p) 0 1)
   (ite (= n2_p n3_p) 0 100)))
(minimize imbalance)
(check-sat)
```

The other engines produce the expected results.

A quick pass through GDB points to `model_evaluator::model_evaluator(model_core&, params_ref const&)` as the source of the segfault for bcd2.  Stack trace bellow:

issue_title: pyZ3 crashing
issue_body:
In ref to a previous ticket (https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/issues/485), I've again stumbled into a situation that regularly causes python to completely crash. Here's how to reproduce:

git clone https://github.com/Owlz/pySym/tree/z3_crashing

Ensure you have z3 available for import (workon/virtualenv/etc).

Open ipython (v3)

```python
import logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG,format='%(name)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s', datefmt='%m/%d/%Y %I:%M:%S %p')
from pyPath import Path
import ast
import Colorer
from pyPathGroup import PathGroup

source = """
a = 1
"""

b = ast.parse(source).body
p = Path(b,source=source)
pg = PathGroup(p)
pg.explore()
pg.errored[0].step()
```

You can also usually replace that last step command with simply attempting to exit out of the ipython interpreter. Usually you get a general core dump message or "corrupted double-linked list: 0x00000000015d7150" type message.

EDIT: This problem re-appeared when attempting to implement s.translate in my pyState/__init__.py:copy routine.

issue_title: No theory of floating point, bitvectors and arrays and mixed performance?
issue_body:
Consider the following queries (I think they are technically equi-satisfiable). From the perspective of the work that I'm doing I can think of them as being equivalent.

### Using floating point variables (execution time < 1 second)

```
; start Z3 query
; Emited by klee::Z3SolverImpl::getConstraintLog()
; Rewritten by hand to not use arrays.

(set-logic QF_FPBV)
(declare-fun x() (_ FloatingPoint 11 53))
(assert (not (fp.isNaN x)))
(assert (not (fp.isInfinite x)))

(assert
  (fp.eq
    (fp.sub roundNearestTiesToEven
      (fp.mul roundNearestTiesToEven
        x
        ((_ to_fp 11 53) (_ bv4617315517961601024 64))
      )
      ((_ to_fp 11 53) (_ bv4621819117588971520 64))
    )
    ((_ to_fp 11 53) (_ bv0 64))
  )
)


(assert
  (not
    (fp.eq
      x
      ((_ to_fp 11 53) (_ bv4611686018427387904 64))
    )
  )
)
(check-sat)
; end Z3 query
```

### Using arrays of bitvectors instead of  (execution time: ~ 5 seconds)

```
; start Z3 query
; Emited by klee::Z3SolverImpl::getConstraintLog()
(set-info :status unknown)
(declare-fun x_0x2080d70 () (Array (_ BitVec 32) (_ BitVec 8)))
(assert
 (let ((?x9 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv2 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv1 32)) (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv0 32))))))
 (let ((?x29 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv4 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv3 32)) ?x9))))
 (let ((?x12 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv6 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv5 32)) ?x29))))
 (let ((?x30 ((_ to_fp 11 53) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv7 32)) ?x12))))
 (not (fp.isNaN ?x30)))))))
(assert
 (let ((?x9 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv2 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv1 32)) (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv0 32))))))
 (let ((?x29 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv4 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv3 32)) ?x9))))
 (let ((?x12 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv6 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv5 32)) ?x29))))
 (let ((?x30 ((_ to_fp 11 53) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv7 32)) ?x12))))
 (not (fp.isInfinite ?x30)))))))
(assert
 (let ((?x9 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv2 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv1 32)) (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv0 32))))))
 (let ((?x29 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv4 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv3 32)) ?x9))))
 (let ((?x12 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv6 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv5 32)) ?x29))))
 (let ((?x30 ((_ to_fp 11 53) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv7 32)) ?x12))))
 (let ((?x143 (fp.sub roundNearestTiesToEven (fp.mul roundNearestTiesToEven ?x30 ((_ to_fp 11 53) (_ bv4617315517961601024 64))) ((_ to_fp 11 53) (_ bv4621819117588971520 64)))))
 (fp.eq ?x143 ((_ to_fp 11 53) (_ bv0 64)))))))))
(assert
 (let ((?x9 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv2 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv1 32)) (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv0 32))))))
(let ((?x29 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv4 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv3 32)) ?x9))))
(let ((?x12 (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv6 32)) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv5 32)) ?x29))))
(let ((?x30 ((_ to_fp 11 53) (concat (select x_0x2080d70 (_ bv7 32)) ?x12))))
(not (fp.eq ?x30 ((_ to_fp 11 53) (_ bv4611686018427387904 64)))))))))
(check-sat)
; end Z3 query

```

There is a noticeable performance difference between the two, does anyone know why?

I tried looking for a logic for "arrays of bitvectors and floating point arithmetic" logic in Z3's code so I could set the logic but I couldn't find one. Is there one?

issue_title: reduce_args tactic produces models with a default value only
issue_body:
The reduce_args tactic's model converter isn't playing very nicely. See the following example:

```Python
from z3 import *
f = Function('f', IntSort(), IntSort())

s = AndThen(Tactic('reduce-args'), Tactic('smt')).solver()
s.add(Or(f(1) == 2, f(2) == 3))
s.check()
print s.model()

s = Tactic('smt').solver()
s.add(Or(f(1) == 2, f(2) == 3))
s.check()
print s.model()
```

Outputs:
```
[f = [else -> If(Var(0) == 2, 0, 2)]]
[f = [1 -> 2, 2 -> 0, else -> 2]]
```

It would be nicer to get the map as we do without using the tactic, since then the API works properly (rather than potentially having to parse the "default" expression).

issue_title: Update mnist example to support step-by-step learning and remove inversion of control
issue_body:
I'd like to improve mnist example to avoid unnecessary complication and allow easier reusability.

Currently we do:
```python
def train(iter_funcs, dataset, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE):
    for epoch in itertools.count(1):
        ..do training...
        yield result

for epoch in train(iter_funcs, dataset):
    print epoch
```
Instead I'd like to see:
```python
def train_epoch(iter_funcs, dataset, batch_size=BATCH_SIZE):
    ..do training...
    return result

for epoch in itertools.count(1):
    result = train_epoch(iter_funcs, dataset)
    print result
```
The current way of doing it looks like an unnecessary complication to me, especially for the people who want to feed network with a lot of data in macro batches that don't fit into memory.

I think the library-like continuous learning concept
```python
some_more_data, some_more_labels = grab_more_data_and_labels()
network.learn(some_more_data, some_more_labels)
```
is more general and scalable, compared to framework-like learning-once concept:
```
network.learn_until_done(all_data, all_labels)
```

issue_title: Tool specific misc option - refactoring
issue_body:
Connected to #150, and #149 . The parsin g helps to understand the xml format of tools better which will lead to unification of misc options.

An example what I have at the moment for IAR and uVision.

```
iar:
        misc:
            ICCARM:
                data:
                    option:
                        CCDebugInfo:
                            - enable
                        CCOptLevel:
                            - 1
                        IccLang:
                            - 1
                        IccCppDialect:
                            - 2
            General:
                data:
                    option:
                        GRuntimeLibSelect:
                            - 2
            AARM:
                data:
                    option:
                        ACaseSensitivity:
                            - enable
                        ADebug:
                            - enable
```

and uvision:

```
        misc:
            Cads:
                MiscControls:
                    - --debug -g --gnu
                Ropi:
                    - enable
                Optim:
                    - 1
                uC99:
                    - enable
                OneElfS:
                    - enable
```

The result should be that these misc options are as close as to the xml format. This will be another v0.6 breaking change, but if we do it right, should stay for a while :smile: The misc will be supported,the template will be easy-to-use option, as this misc needs some knowledge about all available option.

issue_title: Adding sweep based information
issue_body:
This is a POC design we've come up during the hackathon at the allen institute.

I'm including it here so that it does not get lost and avoid duplicating work. I will very likely not have the time to pursue an implementation for that in the near future.

# Problem

In Icephys it is common to have sweeps (a group of PatchClampSeries belonging
together, were up to two TimeSeries are from one electrode, including
TimeSeries not related to an electrode (aka TTL channels)).

Currently the concept of sweeps is not present in NWB so linking different
TimeSeries together is not possible.

# Solution

Add some more tables

## Sweep Table

|Sweeps  | Multiple links to rows in the electrode table   | Multiple links to TimeSeries (TTL channels)   |
|------- | ----------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
|        |                                                 |                                               |

### HDF5 Layout

1D-Array of a compound


## IC Electrode Table

 |id | Soft Link to device | Name | ... | Stimulus (Soft link to TimeSeries) | Response (Soft link to TimeSeries) |
 |---|---------------------|------|-----|------------------------------------|------------------------------------|
 |   |                     |      |     |                                    |                                    |
 
### HDF5 Layout

1D-Array of compound

## Related changes

PatchClampSeries objects will have an electrode region reference indexing into the electrode table.

# Things to clarify and check

- How do we handle the varying number of links in the sweep table?
- Can we append (in h5py and Igor Pro) to arrays of compounds?
- Is that interesting for non-icephys areas as well?

issue_title: Branches that leave the function are not always clickable
issue_body:
Consider the following small program:

```
global _start

_start:
  call main

main:
  mov rdi, 42
  mov rax, 60
  syscall
  ret
```

When loading that program into `angr-management`, the `_start` function is displayed like this:

![pic](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3192959/37642571-df4dbc9c-2c1d-11e8-87ec-4792a28f0281.png)

Note that the `main` reference is not clickable (it would have a different color if it was clickable).

The reason for this is that when building the supergraph, edges to blocks outside of the current function are removed. When we later register the out branches of each node, the `_start` node no longer has any outgoing edges (since they got removed in the first step).

It only works correclty if `main` is identified as a `FunctionNode`, but not all branch targets that leave the current function lead to a `FunctionNode`.

issue_title: AsyncWeChatClient 中 fetch_access_token 方法异步化
是否应该 reraise requests.RequestException 为 WeChatClientException
issue_body:
目前的设计，在调用 ``WeChatClient`` 的相关接口时，不会去捕获 ``requests.RequestException``，而是使用了 ``raise_for_status`` 方法在 API status_code 为非 2xx 的情况下自动 raise ``requests.RequestException``.

https://github.com/jxtech/wechatpy/blob/master/wechatpy/client/base.py#L63

如此导致在调用接口时，不仅要捕获 ``WeChatClientException`` 还需要考虑捕获 ``requests.RequestException``，增加了调用者的负担。

可能的设计是将 ``requests.RequestException`` 捕获并 reraise 为 ``WeChatClientException``，并对 ``WeChatClientException`` 增加 ``request`` 和 ``response`` 属性。参考类似实现：

https://github.com/jxtech/teambition-api/blob/master/teambition/client.py#L137

issue_body:

1. Unchecking it from arming checks list in the standard parameters
2. Setting AHRS_GPS_USE to 0 and Arming check to -9 in the full parameters list.

Problem: Now when I try to arm the drone using the conventional method as shown in the dronekit python documentation it shows: "Prearm: Need 3D fix". However, if I arm using my RC controller, it arms.  This is somehow pointing to the fact that somehow dronekit-python still performs a GPS 3D fix check even if we set it in the mission planner to ignore the GPS check. I am not sure if this is the case, but my experiments are pointing towards this fact. Hence, can somebody kindly provide some insight into this matter. issue_title: Join user and package area files
issue_body:
Currently the `parse_area_file` function only allows one single file. This causes limitations in software like `satpy` where we may want to have a "user" area file and a "package" area file. The easiest/cleanest way to handle this is to make a pyresample allow more than one file. This is even more useful for the new yaml files which could allow users area files to "update" existing information in the "package" area file.

I propose that there be a new function (for backwards compatibility) that accepts one of the following input formats for the areas files:

1. single area file name
2. single file-like object
3. single file content string
4. iterable of area file name or file-like object

issue_title: `asText` needs to do some re-escaping of path segments
issue_body:
```
>>> a = hyperlink.URL.fromText("urn%3Aietf%3Awg%3Aoauth%3A2.0%3Aoob")
>>> b = hyperlink.URL.fromText(a.asText())
>>> a == b
True
>>> c = hyperlink.URL.fromText(a.asIRI().asText())
>>> c == a
False
>>> c == b
False
```

If there are colons (maybe other characters too) in the path segment of a relative URI, they need to be escaped on output; `.asText()` should never produce a syntactically invalid URI.

issue_title: Making sure there are kerberos tickets in notebooks
issue_body:
For those who may want to use Kerberized livy, you need to make sure Kerberos credentials are available for users. Here's what we did: krb5.conf, because of limitations to python implementation of kerberos can't use KEYRING 
default_ccache_name = /tmp/krb5cc_%{uid}
noaddresses = true
At the beginning of jupyterhub.conf, make sure the KRB5CCNAME environment variable is set 
import pwd
import grp
import jupyterhub

# extend the spawner to add KRB5CCNAME. The call to PAM auth sets up the credential cache,                                                      
# put we need it in KRB5CCNAME so renewd will know to renew it                                                                                  

from jupyterhub.spawner import LocalProcessSpawner

class LocalProcessSpawnerEnv(jupyterhub.spawner.LocalProcessSpawner):

    def get_env(self):
        env = super().get_env()
        env['KRB5CCNAME'] = '/tmp/krb5cc_' + str(pwd.getpwnam(self.user.name).pw_uid)
        return env

c.JupyterHub.spawner_class = LocalProcessSpawnerEnv
c.PAMAuthenticator.open_sessions = True We use sssd for authentication, but pam_krb5 or anything else is fine as long as it creates a credential cache in /tmp/krb5cc_UID. (If it goes somewhere else, make the obvious adjustment to the python code above.) We make one other change, but it's specific to our site. We have a process that automatically renews credentials as long as the user is logged in. (It's available in github.com/clhedrick/kerberos) That requires us to call a custom pam module. In jupyterhub.conf c.PAMAuthenticator.service = 'jupyter' In /etc/pam.d/jupyter 
#%PAM-1.0
auth       include      login
auth       required     pam_reg_cc.so usecollection
account    include      login
password   include      login
session    include      login

issue_title: dont use $(solutiondir) in project settings realsense.vcxproj
issue_body:
| Required Info  |   |
|---|---|
| Camera Model | R200, F200 and SR300 | 
| Firmware Version |   | 
| Operating System & Version |  Windows  |
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)  |   |
| Build System |  VS2015 | 

When you use $(solutiondir) to configure dependencies and build paths in visual studio this may cause problems when using that project to other solutions.   for example, someone who has projects that depend on the realsense library will likely add realsense.vcxproj to their own solution and add a project dependency causing a convenient rebuild when librealsense is updated etc.  Because solutiondir will be different, it will affect how librealsense gets compiled (or fail to get compiled).  

Currently in librealsense.vc14/realsense/realsense.vcxproj there are 4 such entries in the form of:
```
   <ModuleDefinitionFile>$(SolutionDir)../CMake/realsense.def</ModuleDefinitionFile>
```
a better approach would be to use:
```
    <ModuleDefinitionFile>../../CMake/realsense.def</ModuleDefinitionFile>
```

issue_title: When a server uses threads, it loses the context of the context manager
issue_body:
If a server uses threads, i.e. to send requests concurrently to other servers, the threads lose the context stored in the context manager caller thread.
This means the threads won't have the context stored by the context manager, and they'll open a new trace.

issue_title: Multi-resource locking
issue_body:
Not sure exactly how to test this out but basically, here's the use case:

1. Create a repo and an importer
2. Sync the repo and importer
3. Try to update the repo or the importer during the sync
4. The update shouldn't happen until after the sync

Another use case:

1. Create a repo and an importer
2. Update the importer to point to a different url
3. Immediately run a sync
4. The sync should fire after the update and sync from the second url

Using python 3.6, linux+mac

I'm trying to fetch data via http but the nested call hangs - this can be replicated by using `asyncio.sleep` - I'm guessing it might have to do with graphene? *or core?*
*(or my lack of python experience - being a node dev I might have the wrong mindset)*

Replication: *comment out `asyncio.sleep` in `fakeFetch` to see the difference*
```python
from flask import Flask
from flask_graphql import GraphQLView
from graphql.execution.executors.asyncio import AsyncioExecutor
import graphene
import asyncio
import sys

app = Flask(__name__)

def Log(message):
    print(f"LOG: {message}", file=sys.stderr)

async def fakeFetch():
    Log('Wait a sec...')
    await asyncio.sleep(1) # Comment this out and things work
    return 'Async World'

class Bars(graphene.ObjectType):
    bar = graphene.String()
    async def resolve_bar(self, args, context, info):
        await fakeFetch()
        return 'Bar'

class Foo(graphene.ObjectType):
    bars = graphene.Field(Bars)
    async def resolve_bars(self, args, context, info):
        await fakeFetch()
        return Bars()

class Query(graphene.ObjectType):

schema = graphene.Schema(query=Query)

if __name__ == "__main__":

issue_title: Smoothed curves have a kink in them
issue_body:
After upgrading to Tensorboard 1.0.0a6 (from PyPI), I started seeing kinks in smoothed graphs. These give the impression that the value suddenly changed at a much faster rate, but in fact the kinks are an artefact of the smoothing algorithm; the kink moves with the smoothing factor:

![smoothing-900-circle](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/506953/32542059-c4d12408-c471-11e7-9296-9f98bab5ef03.png)
![smoothing-925-circle](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/506953/32542062-c4e6d2da-c471-11e7-9638-51f25b8dcb15.png)
![smoothing-95-circle](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/506953/32542058-c4bd8218-c471-11e7-83f0-02eee5a237e5.png)

From top to bottom, a smoothing factor of 0.9, 0.925, and 0.95.

I did not see those kinks with the version I used before. Unfortunately I have no record of which version I was using before 1.0.0a6. In that version the tail of the curve was also smoothed, unlike in 1.0.0.0a6 where the last smoothed point coincides with the last data point.
